name: Asbill, Clarence Monroe
motto: Neither to laugh nor to cry, but to understand.
clubs: Pistol and Machine Gun Sharpshooter, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: 'C.M.' is one of those steady, quiet, straightforward fellows that gradually makes a place for himself in the hearts of all who know him. He came to Clemson with the determination to fathom the mysterious realm of the electron. His efforts have been gradually rewarded, for not only has he obtained a technical education, but he has made for himself a host of friends whose esteem he may carry forever. Truly a man's college career has been successful when he has proven himself a scholar, a friend, and a gentleman. Clarence's maneuvers in respect to the fairer sex have been something of a mystery. He is not a 'woman hater', though, as some fair damsel can probably testify. Those that wait the longest fall the hardest; so 'C.M.' is likely in for quite a bump.

name: Ayers, David Cox
motto: It matters not how long we live, but how.
clubs: Member Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Club
Member Beaux Arts Institute of Design '24-'25
description: Bright lights, swaying figures, bewitching music, or a palette, paint, and brush—such are dear to the heart of D. C. This boy shoves a wicked pencil; he can draw anything from an Arabian Mosque to an ace of spades. Doubtless, in later years we shall hear of some sensation he has created in the Latin Quarter or Greenwich Village. For in June, D. C. sails for gay 'Paree'; whether to study Architecture or capture hearts we do not know, but, judging from past experiences in Anderson, we are inclined to think the latter. D. C. has made many friends at Clemson and on the 'Hill.' A true friend, a good worker, and a good student, D. C. has shown his ability in Architecture and we prophesy a brilliant future for him.

name: Cary, Francis Linden
motto: To obtain success through the pursuit of the most worthy ideals.
clubs: Senior Literary Critic and Recording Secretary of Columbian Literary Society
Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Among the three hundred and thirty-eight "new boys" who matriculated at Clemson in the fall of '21 was a young lad from Seneca—Francis Cary, and well may the "ole home town" be proud of him. Aspiring to no military honors, he joined the band and there won distinction by being the leading man in the piccolo section for four years. "Teater" chose to be a disciple of "Crip," and has allowed nothing to come between him and the mastery of his course in Electrical Engineering. Indeed, this is one of his outstanding traits, the ability to stick to a thing until he has accomplished his purpose. He is quiet and unassuming, and by his pleasing personality has made scores of friends at Clemson. Above all, he is a man. The class of '25 wishes you the greatest of success, Cary. A continuance of those traits which have characterized your college career is bound to bring achievements which will be a credit to your class and to your Alma Mater.

name: Causey, Luther Gordon
motto: What a man holds behind his teeth hurts neither himself nor anyone else.
clubs: Machine Gun Expert With Highest Score, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Cueball" immigrated to Clemson from the independent republic of Horry in the fall of 1921 and although his folks, during his college years have moved over the border into Tarhelia, he is still a loyal South Carolinian at heart. "L. G." is a thorough student. Not a "plugger," as at times we have suspected—but one who believes in "understanding all he knows about it"; and he has made an enviable record in scholarship which ranks him with the highest men in his class. "L. G." has concentrated his energies to the seeking of Horticultural knowledge. He is possessed with the idea of transforming his native state into a veritable garden of Eden—to strew the land with fruit—an orchard on every country place to add to the satisfaction of life, and enhance the charm of the open country. And, if a wish will help you on your way, may you succeed in that, "Cueball," my lad!

name: Chandler, Jack Willard
motto: Work as though you were to live forever—Live as though you were to die tomorrow.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Jack came sprinting to us in the fall of 1921 with the same dash that was to show up later against many track and basketball teams. It would take several volumes to express the esteem with which we hold Jack. The class of '25 would be incomplete without him. Always cheerful, and with a ready smile, he is one of the most popular members of the class. He justly deserves the high place he holds in the student body, for his sterling character, mental worth, and natural ability have been recognized by everyone with whom he has come in contact. Jack easily masters his different studies and has always stood high in his scholastic work. Here's to you, "old boy", may you always be as good a man in life as you have been in college.

name: Chappell, Irvin Walker
motto: When a man hasn't anything to say, that's the best time to say it.
description: "Chap" decided that the village of Jenkinsville could do without its champion whittler for a while; so he cut a new pair of grape-vine suspenders and headed for the hills. We think that he was inspired to take mechanical engineering by the whining and wheezing of the Blue Ridge locomotive that brought him on his journey as far as Cherry's. His greatest characteristic is determination. He wouldn't let even a broken leg keep him from stealing second base in his first game of varsity baseball. He is quiet and unassuming; but "still waters run deep," and we are expecting great things of this lad in the future. Luck to you, "Chap"; may the best of life be yours.

name: Cobb, Wade Hampton
motto: When the great scorer comes to write against your name, He writes not that you won or lost, but how you played the game.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Richland County Club '21
Animal Husbandry Judging Team at Southeastern Fair
Infantry Unit, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Wade, the possessor of an attractive personality and a sunny disposition, has gained a host of friends during his stay at Clemson and has well earned the name of "hail fellow well met." "Ty," is a true Southern gentleman imbued with a high spirit of honor, friendliness to all, high ideals, and lofty ambitions. Wade cast his lot with the "bull" men, and has been an able and efficient student in his chosen line of work. Studies have been the least of his worries, having always been able to "shoot" the professors on all occasions. Wade Hampton, you go from us with a record none can reproach, the love, the esteem, and the admiration, and the best wishes of the entire class—surely success and happiness await you.

name: Colbert, Folsom Hume
motto: Life is a succession of doors to be closed, and never re-opened.
description: During his four-year sojourn with us "Pinkie" has lived well and laughed often. He intends doing much living when he goes back home; thus his success is assured. Besides being a good student, "Pinkie" is a star in track, football, and basketball. He is undaunted by superior opposition, and as captain of the basketball team, his indomitable courage and mere presence has upon repeated occasions inspired his teammates to fight on when there was nothing in them except the will which said to them, "Fight on." One touch of "Pinkie's" humor makes the whole cadet corps grin; but this is no indication that he has no studies, meditations, or superior thoughts, for in reality he has a man's head. But beneath this man's head is a boy's heart—overflowing with all the yearning and eagerness of eternal youth.

name: Coleman, Joe Maud
motto: Evil to him who thinks evil, and good to him who thinks good.
description: This true Tiger comes from the wilds of Saluda County. In the fall of 1921 decided to see the world through a transit. So he selected Clemson for his school, to pursue that course of study. will do what he says he will do. He is a loyal Tiger, a sincere friend, and above all—a gentleman. That outstanding touch of kindness and friendship that we all admire, is certainly a consummation devoutly to be wished by everyone. With all these traits and an excellent character, has the determination to win against any odds. With that spirit of ability he cannot lose. We, as Clemson men, regret very much the losing of this dear friend. However, we believe that success will be his highest ambition in whatever he may undertake. Here's wishing you the best of luck, and success in whatever role you may choose.

name: Coleman, Horace Victor
motto: Do your duty, and do it well; What more can anyone do?
description: In the fall of '21 Silver Street lost one of its fair representatives, stopped over at Clemson for four years in search of a greater world. has a strong determination to make good, and if he fulfills our predictions, he will soon be a successful engineer. has quite a lot of personal magnetism about him, and naturally he has made a host of friends during his stay at Clemson. He stands high in his scholastic work, and never has any difficulty in mastering his studies. He is well-known and admired by both the students and the faculty. In spite of all these fine qualities, has a weak spot that is liable to cause him to fumble. He carries on such a wide correspondence with girls in various towns that we are afraid that his greatest ambition will be the building of a little bungalow somewhere near Silver Street.

name: Perrin, Watson Coleman
motto: Smile and the world smiles with you
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "J. W." was one of the first men in the class of '25 to witness the beauty of the old clock tower as it loomed into view across the hills of Oconee. His purpose was to make college everything that it should be. His success along this line has been marked. His friends are many, true and tried. Those who wish him success, are all who know him. Besides winning the friendship of the cadets, "P. W." has won the admiration of all the "fair sex" who have been lucky enough to know him. Dreams! All great men are susceptible to the malady of dreams. "P.W." began his dream of conquering the textile world September thirteenth, 1921. For four long years he has held fast to his ideals. He has fought and won every battle including the historic struggle of "Organic Chemistry." Now, "P.W.", as we bid the dear old college days adieu, I wish you everything that makes for the better.

name: John, Frank Corbin
motto: If you have built castles in the air, your work is not lost; that is where they should be; now put foundations under them.
description: John believes in the lucky number 13 since five important events occurred to him on the 13th of the month. He enlisted in the army on the thirteenth of June, left camp for port of embarkation on the thirteenth of May and landed at Calais, France, exactly one month later on the thirteenth of June and one year from enlistment. It was on the thirteenth of April, 1919, that he landed back in the U.S., at Charleston, S. C. And he also began his rehabilitation training on April 13, 1921. John expects to teach Agricultural Education and revolutionize farming in his chosen home.

name: Darby, James Edgar
motto: I'd rather have the love of a true girl, than all the riches of the world.
description: "Major's" scholastic record shows that he has chosen a course in which he is deeply interested. He is not only a good student, but he is a strong promoter of all student activities. He has many friends because he always stands up for the right and is willing at all times to lend a helping hand. We would have great aspirations for "Major" but we understand he gets a letter from Pelzer every day. You may never be a Steinmetz, "Major", but we will bet on you being happy in June.

name: Dicks, Reginald Leon
motto: You cannot expect more of life than you put into it.
description: While he has been with us, his rosy cheeks and smiling countenance have won for him many a score of friends within the walls of Tigertown. "Reggie" is an architect. As a devoted disciple of Lange, "Red," and "Doc," he is booked for a brilliant success in the realm of Angelo and Cass Gilbert. With his practical side, his stickability, and his love for work, we can prophesy nothing but success for him. And now, Reginald, at the parting of the ways; we, your pals and fellow students, unite in bidding you God speed in your career. May your success be written big, not only in the building world, but even from the imbedded foundations to the sweeping spires of achievement in the building of your life, may your ideals ever ring true.

name: Edens, George Carrol
motto: Give every man thine ears, but few thy voice; take each man's censure, but reserve thy judgment.
description: Edens answered the call of his country in 1918 and served ten months with the 56th Infantry in France. After returning home, he married, and began farming, but soon realized that he could be of more service to his state by procuring an education first. Although he had been out of school several years, he did not feel handicapped, for he entered the Freshman class in 1920 and by persistent efforts and hard work mastered his course and earned his diploma. Edens has a quiet and reserved disposition, and one more thing we can say of him is that he "tends to his own business." His unassuming manners and sound judgment have won for him the warm friendship of those with whom he has come in contact. We join in wishing you success, Edens, and may your happiness in married life continue.

name: Fletcher, Edwin Gibson
motto: Women are the bread of Life. I'm getting hungry.
description: "Fletch" came to Clemson in the fall of '20, but afterwards decided that there were too many good fellows in the class of '24 to associate with, and cast his lot with our class. "Fletch" thinks that electricity is traveling too slow at present and with the aid of his course in electrical engineering he hopes to electrify the world. We hope some day that he may rival Edison or Marconi, and may be able to call himself a "fair representative" of the on-rushing town of McColl. Not only has this lad made good in his classwork, but his pleasant personality and ready wit have won the admiration and friendship of all who know him. He has proved himself a man worthy of being called a Clemson Tiger. Here's to you, Fletch. As you venture out into life, may Lady Fortune deal you a hand that is worthy of the class-mate you have been.

name: Hoyt, Bethea Flowers
motto: Have more than thou showest and know more than thou sayest.
description: "Buck" came to Clemson in the fall of '21 and ever since that time has been a popular member of our class. Upon arriving at Clemson "Buck" cast his lot among the seekers of fame in the textile world. To this end he has applied himself diligently and is now ready to do his part in the world by making use of his chosen profession. To know "Buck" is to like him, as is evident by his host of friends. He is true to all, doing always what he thinks right and usually thinking correctly. "Buck" is one of those men on whom you can depend to do what he says he will do. He is a friend indeed and never is he too busy to help a friend in need. "Buck" has taken an active part in college activities. This is shown by his untiring work on the "Tiger" staff. Thus, we see that "Buck" has not only been efficient in his class work, but has proved himself a loyal Tiger in many respects and Clemson will feel the loss of him.

name: Barton, Woodward Freeland
motto: Life is but a grindstone, and whether it grinds a man down or polishes him up, depends on the stuff he's made of.
clubs: Student Volunteer Convention Delegate in Indianapolis '23
description: This young man hails from Louisiana. After completing his high school days, he decided to come to South Carolina to finish his education. Barton is an agronomist. Through his strong determination and hard work he has won the esteem of all who know him. He is the constructive type of lad of which Clemson is proud. His wonderful personality and friendly demeanor have won a vast number of friends. Since he has followed the agronomy section he is doomed to grow rice on the prairies of Louisiana. We know that his ability and determination will win for him high achievements.

name: Freeman, William Clarence
motto: I often regret my speech, but never my silence.
description: A self-made man and one who has plenty of worldly experience before finishing his college course, is this loyal Tiger. Freeman entered college handicapped by a lack of preparatory training, but with that same indomitable courage that carried him through his nine months in action with the A.E.F., he has conquered his academic adversaries and is now ready to go out in the world in possession of a college degree. William is one of our ex-soldiers. His army life extends over a period of five years, during which he served eighteen months overseas. Nine of these eighteen months were spent in action, he having participated in fourteen battles. Experience is a dear teacher, and as one of her pupils, Freeman has learned many things that could never be learned at any college. He has made a wonderful success at Clemson and has the prospect of a very bright future before him. He has won many friends and they are united in wishing for him the best that life can give.

name: Gillespie, Bryan Boroughs
motto: The motto of chivalry is also the motto of wisdom; serve all, but love only one.
clubs: Member Sophomore and Junior Honor Councils
Recording Secretary and President Columbian Literary Society
Winner of Debater's Medal, Columbian Literary Society
First Class Gunner, Machine Gun Company, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: The little city of Seneca gave up a worthy son when "B Square" decided to enter Clemson in the fall of 1921. Since that time he has gained many friends and proved himself to be an earnest worker. He has done much for his Alma Mater and his fellow students. After deciding that Electrical Engineering would be his major course, he determined to make this first stepping stone of his life a success. As a result he has been a consistent "E" man, shooting all his professors and leading the class for four years. "Buck" is a deserving and loyal son of Clemson; he is capable, reliable, and in every respect a gentleman. And with all seriousness, we can say that he is an intelligent, hard and consistent worker. These qualities, being the mettle of real men, success for him is assured.

name: Babb, Jack
motto: Things worth doing at all, are worth doing well.
description: We wish to present Jack, the boy with auburn hair from Pelzer. Jack cast his lot with the Tigers in '21. For two years he gave many a dancer a treat by "tickling the ivory" in the Tiger Jungaleers. He was not content to be only a second Paderewski, but he wished to be a rival of Edison, so he chose Electrical Engineering as his course. He also was a member of the cadet band and pumps a "bass" almost as efficiently as he chases electrons. This lad is an A-1 boy and has won for himself a host of friends who will always remember him with the deepest affection. "Brass-head", as he is often called, is a favorite among the boys as well as among the fairer sex. So here's to Jack, wherever you may go, or whatever you may do, we wish you the greatest of success and trust that you will always maintain your magnetic personality.

name: Bagwell, John Callahan
motto: Let us forget the things of the past and press forth into the things of the future.
clubs: Member of "Dead-Beats" Club, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In the fall of '21 there ventured forth out of Honea Path this bright young lad, Johnny, though vivacious and witty, is sober-minded and sincere. He is a straightforward, honest, and hard-working fellow, and never shuns the call of duty. The promotion to sergeant in his junior year won him the name of "Sarge." "Sarge's" big heart, sympathetic nature, and winning personality, coupled with his regard for truth and honesty have won for him the respect of the entire class, who unite in wishing for him the best life can give.

name: Goodale, Thomas Edwin
motto: Give me a bowl of wine; 'tis sweeter than a maiden's kiss.
clubs: R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala., Expert Pistol Shot
description: Sh, Listen!! To the story of a cheery lad who came to Clemson with great ambitions but went down like the flood. He is a true friend, good sport, and a loyal Tiger--what more could be said of a man? He is out for a good time and he will try anything once and trust to luck for results. He loves tennis like a baby loves milk and a few other things. Our best wishes follow you throughout your life. We hope that your ambition will be fulfilled so that in the future you will sit before a crackling fire, pop popcorn, crack hickory nuts and entertain the family.

name: Goree, Irby Major
motto: It's good to be merry and wise; It's good to be honest and true; And before you're off with the old love It's best to be on with the new.
clubs: Rifle and Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C. Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Allow me to present to you. He is a ladies man and can often be seen with some of the "fair sex" by his side. He has a magnetic lure about him. Upon entering Clemson he decided to make Textile Engineering his life work. He was very successful as a "lint-head," but soon found that Industrial Education was better suited for a man of his ability. He has been a consistent maker of good marks in his academic work. He is an earnest worker and has mastered the tasks which have been put before him. To know him is to like him for he has all the merits that go with the making of a gentleman. He has served four successful years with the band and has proved himself to be a good musician as well as a scholar. We wish you the best of luck in your future work. May success be yours in whatever you undertake to do!

name: Graves, Paul
motto: They say true love is a blessing.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Abbeville County Club '24
Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Don't laugh too hard, fellow reader, when you look at the above photograph. Naturally you can't help letting a few guffaws of mirth escape. Clemson has been laughing with, as well as at Paul Graves for four years. The habit is hard to stop. Blessed, they say, is he who spreads good cheer among his fellowmen. Such being the case, this boy from Abbeville is indeed thrice blessed, for he keeps Tigertown roaring with gales of laughter all the time. Those who will, may have their learned scholars, their financial wizards, their soldiers, their statesmen. Give us the man with the hearty laugh, always seeing the bright side of the case. Rise and take the floor, Paul, and may your kind increase. Gloom will be routed and the world will be a better place.

name: Hall, Robert Elliott
motto: Determination makes dreams come true.
description: From the stirring town of Gaffney this young lad burst forth into the limelight of Clemson. It was in the fall of '21 that he fell into our ranks, and the long dream of his life to become an Electrical Engineer began fulfillment. With earnestness and hard work, he has shown forth as a star at night among the electrical group. Hall, or "R.E." as we know him, chose the course he best could follow, and has been a consistent worker. During all his valued moments "R.E." found time for pleasure and time to help his fellow-students. The Electricals are proud to number him in their ranks. Here's to you, "R.E.", may you someday find something in your line that will add to life a blessing and make living a little more worth while.

name: Hart, Thomas Jehu
motto: Nature puts forth her gentlemen, and monarchs must give place.
description: This fair headed boy entered Clemson with but one idea in his head—to make good. Tommy, ole man, our hats are off to you, for you have a college record of which any man might be proud. A wearer of the much coveted block "C" for two years, an honor which comes to only the very select in each class; the vice-presidency of the Y.M.C.A., this honor goes only to a man who is a Christian and a gentleman; the love and admiration of his friends—he has no enemies—this is the reward of a man who does his duty and "plays the game according to its rules" regardless of whether it means win or lose. Tommy you've chosen as your life's work the art of beautifying "Old Mother Earth"; so all your classmates ask of you is that you give to the world, what you gave to your college for four long years—your best!

name: Hawkins, Graham Edward
motto: Anything worth doing at all, is worth doing well.
description: "Hawk-eye" came to us from the thriving town of Timmonsville. Truly Timmonsville lost a fine young lad when he left his home for Tigertown. He cast his lot among the "Dairy-maids" and though not being crowned with "E"s, has done some good work. He showed his "stuff" here by making the judging team at the National Dairy Show held at Milwaukee, Wisconsin. During his stay at Clemson, "Hawk" has made many friends among the professors and cadets as well as among the fairer sex. He has taken his part in the works of the "Y" and has led a Bible Class for three years. When he first came to Clemson, he kept rather close but during his latter two years, he changed into quite a "sheik" among the camper's girls; however, he never forgot the fair one that he left behind. "Hawk", may there be worlds of success in the future for you and yours. We look to you for great things.

name: Harmon, Samuel Ellsworth
motto: Man may hold all sorts of posts, if he'll only hold his tongue.
clubs: Member of Wrestling and Boxing Team
Middle-Weight Champion of Fourth Corps Area
description: The name plate which appears in connection with the above "map" has long since ceased to be representative of the facial terrain for which it was dedicated. A glance at the honors named above is mute evidence of the wide field of activities in which this lad has engaged. The capital city of the Palmetto colony should feel proud to call him her own. To his friends, Harmon is known as a man of sterling character, strong in his convictions, but with it all he is a prince of a good fellow. A gentleman and a scholar is Harmon.

name: Hayes, Baxter Manley
motto: Hatred stirreth up strifes; but love covereth all sins.
description: Through a great amount of study and hard work, Baxter has successfully kept pace with the excess work heaped upon his shoulders, and has earned the right to be called a true son of Clemson. Entering college after a lapse of several years from his school work, which time was partly spent with the American Expeditionary Force in its fight for democracy, Baxter showed his mettle by remastering the fundamentals, and by conquering the advanced elements of that indefinite quality known as Education. If he shows the same determination and desire to succeed after he leaves Clemson, he can do no other than achieve greatness.

name: Haynesworth, Charles Riley
motto: Postpone your worries until tomorrow and you'll have none today.
description: Charles is a product of Sumter. He came to Clemson in the fall of 1921 and decided to cast his lot with the Textile. In the class room he has done much to his credit. If hard work and ability make for anything in life, he will make a success and we are confident that when weighed in the balance, he will not be found wanting. He is a true friend and a lad of unusually good disposition. During his four years at Clemson he has made many friends who wish him the best of luck in life. Charles has a peculiar fondness for Anderson and "Her," and he spends many week-ends in the nearby metropolis. It is whispered that he has a serious case of "heart trouble." Anyway, the balmy air around Anderson seems to act as a tonic. Charles, old boy, whatever you undertake to do, you have our best wishes for a life of happiness and success.

name: Heller, John Roderick
motto: The world looks brighter from behind a smile.
description: In "Rod," we find one of the most substantial members of the Band. He has risen from the rank of private to captaincy of the best military band in the state. His magnetic personality has won for him a host of friends. "Rod's" wide field of activity speaks only slightly of his numerous achievements. His ideals have been of the very highest and he has won for himself a name worthy of the strongest friendship. As a student, he is a diligent worker; he does not know the word failure. The people of Seneca should lift their hats to "Rod" because he has not only achieved the heights of military renown, but he is a man par excellence when it comes to scholastic duties. There will always be a tender feeling in our hearts for you, "Rod," when you depart from our midst to study medicine. Just continue as a diligent student, a loyal Tiger and a man. We predict for you a life of happiness and boundless success.

name: Henderson, Harold Jasper
motto: Agree if you can; if not, dispute it like a man.
description: Harold did not come from the proverbial "briar patch," but from a little town next door, known to us as Greer. To quench his thirst for knowledge he came to Clemson in the fall of 1921 and joined the "bug" men, and is now an entomologist in the true sense of the word. During his sophomore and junior years Harold's military ambitions would seriously interfere with his morning sleep. Like some other great men, he cast aside his honors and would not accept a high position in his senior year. As a track man, Harold is par excellence. In his sophomore year he earned his letter as a pole vaulter on the Tiger track team. Since that time he has held his own as a vaulter. He is also one of the outstanding literary men of his section, never being satisfied with less than an "F". Harold has only one bad habit. That is winning the hearts of the ladies. He thinks he has one captured now, so all but Mildred may go on undisturbed.

name: Henry, Shala Walter
motto: No man is born into this world whose work is not born with him.
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, and President Columbian Literary Society
Recording Secretary Y. M. C. A.
description: Behold! Another Steinmetz. When "Little Dave" steps out of Clemson into the field of volts and amperes, it will take more than hard luck and disappointment to prevent him from crossing the goal marked "Success." "Dave" is a hard worker, especially in electricity, and it takes a mighty difficult problem to stall him. "Dave's" activities in literary society work have enabled him to "tell the world" what he thinks and what he wants, and have helped him to achieve success with many a fair lady. Here's to you, "Dave," Old Clemson may well be proud of you. May you stick to your chosen work and be as successful in that as you have been at college.

name: David, Oscar Herbert
motto: To know, to esteem, to love—and then to part, Makes up Life's tale to many a feeling heart.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Orangeburg County Club
description: D.O. is one of those fortunate young men that the girls just can't resist. He carries this responsibility with becoming dignity, however, and never lets business interfere with pleasure. They say that brains and good looks are never found together, but David is a living denial of that wise crack. It is well known to Campus folks that "Pete" takes his afternoon exercise by climbing the Hotel hill, and no one is surprised to hear that he has smashed feminine hearts galore. He has devoted a proper part of his college years to the pursuit of knowledge, feeling safe in the assurance that honor and fame come to him who acts well his part. We feel sure that his ability and energy will bring him every success in the future, and we send with him the hope that every happiness will be his.

name: Edward, Samuel Herlong
motto: A rare compound of oddity, frolic, and fun To relish a joke and rejoice at a pun.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
Expert Machine Gunner, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Chemists are not always born, sometimes they're made through hard work. This is "Eddie's" case. He came to the lair of the Tiger with many other ambitious young fellows who are anxious to obtain an education. Analytical work became one of his favorite specialties. He has conquered and subdued many unknowns, one of these being the complexities of life. "Ed" has analyzed life and found that a smile, a glad hand, and a ready wit keep away worry and bring unto one innumerable friends. "To love and win is the best thing—To love and lose is the next best." is "Ed's" theory of love, and he is invulnerable to the wiles of the ladies. At times, it seemed that one of his "affaires du coeur" would detract him from his work, but not so, for he has the same idea that many great men have and that is: "Work first and love afterwards."

name: Holmes, Alester Garden, Jr.
motto: Let there be justice, though the heavens should fall.
description: Brought up under the shadow of Clemson, is indeed "Tiger Bred." A true Tiger he is in every sense of the word. For six months of his Sophomore year he tried barracks life in Jeff McMahan's dashing "D" Company, but he has been a day cadet most of his college career. Despite this freedom to roam at will, retains the distinction, rare among Clemson cadets, of having failed to succumb to the "wiles that in women are." Mechanical Engineering is his chosen profession, and there is little doubt that the powerful molecules of steam and the throbbing engines of industry will jump cheerfully at his command.

name: Holmes, Joseph Simpson
motto: Success in life is determined by what you do, not by what you say.
description: Set sail from Mountville in the fall of '21—the moment he hit Tigertown he became one of us. Has chosen Animal Husbandry as the field of work in which to expand his overflow of energy. Judging by the enthusiasm and ability which he has put to play while mastering his profession, we predict that he will overcome all obstacles in later life and sweep to the pinnacle of success. This lad's optimism and ever-ready laugh have drawn all of his associates very close to him. They have learned to admire the firm stand he has taken in upholding his convictions, to value his loyalty and honesty, and to respect his reverence for God. We can pay no greater tribute than to say that he is a prince of a good fellow, a true friend, a loyal Tiger, and a good sport.

name: Huffman, Waldo Carroll
motto:
Why for tomorrow do men stay
When all of life is lived today?
description: "Huff" began his career with the class of '24, but soon learned he was in the wrong class and took a year's vacation. He joined us again in 1923 and has seemed very contented ever since, making friends as the days go by. He cast his hat in the ring with the dairymen and has been very successful in his choice. He is an apt pupil and is much liked by the faculty and students, as well as by the fairer sex—this is his greatest weakness. He leaves his mark in whatever he undertakes. "Huff" has been a loyal "Tiger," taking part in many college activities and working for the advancement of his Alma Mater. If he continues his record as he has begun, we are sure that he will accomplish great things. So remember, "Huff," every member of the class of '25 wishes you the greatest success.

name: Hutto, David Felder
motto: Determination makes dreams come true.
description: Lo and behold! Here he is, folks, a native son, no, not of California, but of Denmark, the saw-mill district of South Carolina. "Pluto" came to Clemson in the fall of '21 from Bailey Military Institute and since then has more than made good in all his work and recreation, including the far-famed autocratic form of military government at Clemson. We are expecting him to enlist in the army as his life's work. This work will suit him fine because he is the Bantam rooster of the class of '25. He is small in stature but more than makes up for it in his strong determination and will-power. Some wise man said, "Brains will rule the world." If so, "Pluto" will at least be in the court. He has lost a good home in Columbia, but his strong determination and will-power have proven the Salvation Army Motto, "You can't keep a good man down." So here's to you, "Pluto." We are looking for great things from you.

name: Bailes, William Brevard
motto: It's the songs ye sing and the smiles ye wear, That's a-makin' the sun shine everywhere.
description: "Tom" is big from all directions; big feet, body, intellect, and heart are only a few ways in which he excels. His unwavering adherence to high principles and altruistic tendency makes it a delight for one to be in his neighborhood. Naturally he has a large place in the hearts of those who know him. As an athlete "Tom" has made good. His enthusiasm and love for football has been rewarded; he is a proud wearer of a block "C" in that great sport. "Tom's" ardent admiration for ebony colored hair leads us to believe that ultimately he will be attracted to tropical regions; and there, under skies powdered with millions of stars and bordered with silent trees, a maid with studied simplicity will be so fascinating as to lure him to a dim veranda, lighted only by the glimmer of the pale starlight. Bewitching glances and soft replies will demolish the walls of his heart's citadel, and the rest will be in the hands of Fate. We hope all your yearnings will be satisfied, "Tom."

name: Barrs, Thomas Albert
motto: Life is what one makes it.
description: Thomas holds no military honors bestowed by this institution, but the honors bestowed upon him while in the service of his country on the Mexican border and in France are those which we should remember him by—they are far more honorable and greater than he could ever have attained here. In the fall of '19, at the end of his career as a soldier, he came to Clemson to satisfy his longing for more knowledge. With him he brought what very few bring—a wife—a fine little lady she is. He entered the vocational training department where he worked faithfully until he was prepared to enter the Freshman class in '21. We have found him to be a friend and a gentleman. We know that he has worked hard while here, for in the face of many obstacles which he has come in contact with, he has made a wonderful record. Thomas, we feel sure that success alone awaits you as we find you laboring in the experimental field of animal industry.

name: Jackson, Sam Lucius
motto: Words without action are like smiling at a woman in the dark.
clubs: Freshman, Sophomore, Junior and Senior Bible Classes
description: This big-hearted, good-natured lad hails from Starr, and this is probably one reason why he has been such a shining light on the gridiron for four years, where he earned the title of "Stonewall" Jackson. He was picked for the all-state team two consecutive years, and the all-Southern in 1923. During his stay at Clemson "Jack" has won the esteem and friendship of all who have known him, professors and fellow-students alike, by his amiable disposition and nobility of character. "Jack" cast his lot as a "Bull" man on entering Clemson, and since that time he has accumulated a great store of knowledge of the animal side, and a wonderful degree of efficiency on the verbal side of the subject. Judging from the length and frequency of letters to Due West we believe that he is seriously contemplating erecting a bungalow for two. We predict nothing other than success for you, "Jack."

name: Jacobi, Lee Rothschild William
motto: All men eat, but only a few know the true flavor of courage.
description: He hails from the town of tall buildings—this lad with the cheerful countenance. Four long years ago he severed the ties which bound him to "The Textile Center of the South" and came falteringly to Clemson to knock at the doors of higher learning. During his sojourn here, "Javelin Jake," as he is known to us, has made himself universally known as a lover of sports, from galloping dominoes down to chess, and has been an ardent aspirant for a berth on several athletic teams. However, his outstanding success has been in the shooting gallery, where his deadly aim has made him famous. "Jake's" one outstanding hobby is photography. He is even seen on the athletic field and around the campus shooting plays or scenes that are of interest. Fun-loving, care-free and full of life, "Jake" is well fitted for the work he has chosen.

name: Francis Cornwell Jennings
motto: You can't have too much of a good thing.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: "Dicey," as all the boys know him, hails from Winnsboro, a place famous for its football stars, Charlie Robinson and Dutch Tennant. This lad's exciting experiences with automobiles—Chevrolets and Paiges in particular—aroused in him a consuming desire to know something about the science of Mechanical Engineering. He, therefore, in the fall of '21 arrived at Tigertown where he began to accumulate the knowledge he desired. "Dicey" has been a hard and consistent worker, and his four years here have been filled with various kinds of activities. He has been a loyal Tiger and has made many friends among the fair sex, and from the numerous letters he receives, we have a strong suspicion that a sweet little girl is waiting for him. Here's luck to you "Dicey," with your persistence and determination we can predict only a successful future for you.

name: Charles Stanley Johnson
motto: The right way is the best way matters not what it may cost.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Rifle Marksman and Pistol Sharpshooter, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This flaxen-haired wanderer, who was first from Sunny Florida, but who now hails from Indiana, has for four years been winning the admiration and confidence of his fellow-classmen. In athletics, his college gave him her highest award, a block letter. In his senior year his teammates and fellow-athletes elected him Secretary of the Block "C" Club. In his everyday college life the men of '25 have wished numerous offices on him, and in each task he has shown his true worth. As you look through the leaves of this book, "Johnnie's" classmates and friends wish you to bear in mind that it was to him that they entrusted the great task of publishing this volume of "Taps." "Johnnie," may you serve the world as you have served Clemson.

name: Jones, George Leroy
motto: If you desire the truth, ask few questions.
clubs: Marksman and Machine Gun Marksman, Camp McClellan
Secretary and Treasurer Marion County Club '25
description: To his many friends he is known as "Monk." He hails from the prosperous city of Mullins, however, any city in the state would be proud to claim him as her own. "Monk" came to Clemson in the fall of '21 with the intention of becoming a horticulturist. He has always been a hard worker, and is always ready to grasp any opportunity that comes his way. He isn't what might be called a sheik, but occasionally he steps out among the ladies, demonstrating what his sheiking qualities might be. During his stay at Clemson "Monk" has made many friends and has accomplished much along the line of his major work. We look forward to you, "Monk" for great things in the future.

name: Keel, Julian Howell
motto: Of fertile mind, of massive heart, He always acts well his part.
clubs: First Class Machine Gunner, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Rusty," realizing the value of a trained mind, gave up the business world and entered Clemson in 1921. He came here with the intention of making good and his record shows that his intentions have been surpassed. A Clemson baseball team without "Rusty" just isn't a team. He has made quite a name for himself and his school in this sport. He is not only valued as an athlete but is held in high esteem as a worthy friend and a gentleman by all who know him. Let us watch "Rusty" in the future for he has a personality all of his own which is bound to give him success, and his sunny disposition and cheerful smile will win him a host of friends. We extend our best wishes to you and hope for you the best life can give.

name: Kibler, John William
motto: A cheerful disposition is a fund of ready capital.
clubs: Pistol Sharpshooter, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Kib" is fond of dancing, but he never allows the waltz and the foxtrot to interfere with his Brownian Movements and other chemical phenomena. "Kib" entered the Chemistry Society in his junior year, and since that time has been a devoted disciple of "Dickie" and General "Nat" (Simpson), being associated also with "Ed" and "Mitch" and "One-Gram." Like Doctor Brackett he is extremely fond of History and Chemistry, and we fear that he will some day unearth a new kind of desiccator "grease" and thus inflict his name upon future students of chemical history. But, be that as it may, "Kib," old fellow, may you find amongst the colloidal intricacies and ashy residues, happiness and success!

name: King, Chester Brooklyn
motto: True happiness comes from making those about us happy.
description: When "Tigertown" opened its doors to admit the class of '25, "C.B." was among those present. He is passing out the same doors, this year, a man of knowledge. Clemson is not the only state school that has a place in "C.B.'s" affections, although, perhaps, the only one of its kind. It is needless to tell those who know him where the other school is located. To say that "C.B." is a prince of a good fellow would be putting it too mild. He has a good nature and a sense of humor, the combination of which is rarely found in one man. We, one and all, think that "C.B." will follow his chief bent—that of making a success of everything he undertakes. We prophesy that in a very few years he will have reached the height of his ambition—conquer the soil. Here's to you, "C.B.", may success, pleasure and happiness be yours always.

name: Kirton, Marion Britton
motto: Ah... but a man's reach must always exceed his grasp!
clubs: Winner of Best Poem Medal, Chronicle '24
description: Found his way out of the wilderness that surrounds his home town, Cades, and came to Clemson in '21 as a faltering freshman to take architecture. In this course he has made a brilliant record, and has not, apparently, found even a limit to his possibilities. His native ability and true artistic temperament have earned him his place as the foremost man in his course. Has not, however, been buried in his work. He has found time to make a host of friends, and a name for himself in many college activities. For you, M.B., there can be nothing but success. Your brilliant record here only presages greater successes in later years. You will be long remembered by your friends, Kit, who unite now in bidding you God speed on that long journey to the fame that awaits you.

name: Knotts, William Thomas
motto: Worry never made men great; why should I worry?
description: Knowledge is power; therefore is a very powerful species of genus homo. He left the fair city of North, S. C., in search of new textile worlds to conquer. Clemson, with its pleasing atmosphere attracted our wandering friend and he decided to try his luck at mastering a course in Textile Engineering. He has a mixture of charm, whole-heartedness, originality, sincerity, neatness, and good lookiness. With such promising traits combined with a determination to win, we can see nothing but a successful future for in this progressive land of ours. Is E pluribus unum of the dear hunters of our class and has obtained the position of the campus sheik. Watch 'em boy, don't follow the same trail all the time. Here's wishing you the best of luck and success.

name: Lee, Roland Linwood Jr.
motto: Honest effort and efficient service will reap their reward this year, as they have every year since time began.
clubs: Manager, Terrible Textiles Basketball and Baseball Teams '24
Pistol and Machine Gun Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: To know Snake is to like him. He is big-hearted and generous, has a fine sense of humor, and is true as steel to those who count themselves his friends. Snake has studied hard and well deserves the good marks he got from his profs. He has not failed to make the most of his four years spent at Clemson. Snake is Peck's Bad Boy of the class of '25. The class would not be complete if it did not have such as he on its roster. A great teaser and always full of mischief, Snake nevertheless, has his serious moments and is conscientious in all his work. Besides participating in many college activities, Snake managed to make a Textile Engineer out of himself. Almost any day he could be found around the Textile Building getting lint in his hair. Snake, a man of your calibre succeeds, and your friends of the class of '25 wish you the best of luck as you set about to overcome the obstacles in your path.

name: Lewis, Jesse Monroe
motto: Be what you endeavor to appear.
description: No. 1 decided to launch his ship on the great ocean of life; realizing that he needed preparation, he set sail towards Clemson. He early formed a liking for plants; and even though tempted to turn away from them, he heard the call of Nature and decided to specialize in Agronomy. Monroe has always been a conscientious and hard worker. He has not found his course a flowery bed of ease; but he has attained high honors for himself. His social affairs never interfere with his studies. While at Clemson, Mon has won for himself the friendship of many members of the student body and faculty. Monroe claims that love is a strange thing, and also that he has always had a liking for strange things. He always finds it convenient to make his journeys by Anderson. We hope you continue the climb to success that you have begun while in college.

name: Lewis, William Marion
motto: I count that man idle that might be better employed.
description: After filling his shoes with sand and putting a plow on the train, they finally got "No. 2" started towards Clemson. The fact that he chose the Mechanical Engineering course is generally conceded to be the result of the inspiration received while working on a wheel-barrow. Marion is a man of unusual ability. He has held a place among the leaders of his class. His pleasing personality has won for him many friends. He generally bears the name of "ladies' man." He has fallen often and hard; but by some mysterious help, he has been able to recover from the hardest of them. His many friends of the state will regret very much when duty calls him to other parts of the world. We believe that great success awaits him a short distance down the path of life.

name: Littlejohn, Charles Milton
motto: 'Tis wise to think what you speak, rather than to speak what you think.
clubs: Charter Member of Clemson College Concert Orchestra '24-'25
Cornetist in Clemson Baptist Church Orchestra '24-'25
Rifle Marksman and Pistol Marksman, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This sterling youth, who is a mathematical shark, began working his head as soon as he entered Clemson, for he elected to blow his way through the Military Department with a horn, and shoot his "profs" with "S's" and "E's". Has he succeeded? He blew himself down to Camp McClellan in the summer of his junior year, and while there, learned to shoot a rifle and a pistol with such accuracy, that upon his return to college, he has shown the danger signal when he missed his mark with a "prof." "Caesar's" ability to toot and shoot, along with his readiness to help a fellow who is down, has made for him many friends. 'Ole fellow, our wish for you is that you have as easy a time holding down the big jobs that life is going to offer you, as you have had in holding the friends that you have made during your four years as a true "Tiger."

name: Long, Charles Amos
motto: "Determination, self-confidence, and effort often make dreams come true."
clubs: Junior Critic, and President, Wade Hampton Literary Society '25
Pistol Marksman R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Charles left Conway in the fall of '21 and came to Clemson with the ambition to master all problems relating to animal production. His efforts have been characterized by an efficiency and enthusiasm which have swept aside all obstacles. Amos has not been a wearer of the block "C", but no truer Tiger supporter ever entered the walls of this noble institution. Behind this young man's unassuming manners there lies strength of character and rare ability. Charlie Amos is a gentleman at all times, has a good word for all and a bad word for none. His jovial disposition, dependability, willingness to serve, and loyalty have greatly endeared him in the hearts of his many friends. The class of '25 is proud to call him her own. Charles, may your future be as successful and rosy as your four years of college life have been! Be ever an inspiration and ideal for your associates, true to yourself, and false to no man. Show your mettle, Amos!

name: Longley, Julian McLaurin
motto: "The faults we see in others are valuable assets when employed to strengthen our own weaknesses."
description: Well folks it's like this, words fail in the attempt to depict this triple-plated prodigy. "Juke" as he is affectionately dubbed, came to Clemson from LaGrange, Ga.; that alone should be a gilt-edge recommendation. His accomplishments in the various phases of college are stepping stones to those which follow. As an athlete, Juke's in a class all by himself. Namely Mexican athletics. Some great man once said, "Great be the man who holds multitudes agog with words of weight" and we claim Juke's all that and more. Success is inevitable to one whose natural ability is so overwhelming and whose failures are even on virtue's side. Here's to you Juke, may your sagacity and connoisseuring qualities put you on top of this old world.

name: Lyons, John Joseph
motto: "Love, hope, fear, faith—these make humanity; these are its sign, and note, and character."
clubs: Member of Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: Anderson is noted for its fine fellows, and Joe is no exception to the rule. He is indeed a paragon for his countless admirers, and his friendship is truly amaranthine. He enjoys dancing, and as dancing and the ladies usually go together, he takes the advantage offered by this fact and indulges in both at the same time. Joe has selected Textile Industrial Education as his course at Clemson, and we wish him the best of luck and the best possible success. His willingness to work and his good disposition will aid him greatly in his struggle for fame and wealth.

name: McCormac, Elias Alford
motto: "There is a past which is gone forever, but there is a future which is still our own."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Dillon County Club '22
description: Elias Alford has stood the test for four years and emerged unharmed except for the loss of most of his hair, which gave him the name of "Pop," and a bruised heart which is about healed. "Mac" finally decided that his native state shouldn't be classed as illiterate; so he joined ranks with "Tsunkiyama" to lift it to a higher level by imparting his knowledge to the younger generations of his future years. In this profession we are sure that he will be an overwhelming success. This boy "Mac," is famous in the classroom and on the dance floor as well as with the girls. But we believe he can now spot ONE for his leading part, and numbers matter no more with him when he goes to sail the matrimonial sea. The world is gaining a real friend in "Mac" and we greatly feel the loss. Look out literary world! Wherever you may go, "Prof," the best wishes of the class of '25 follow you.

name: McCormac, John Hugh
motto: Attempt the end and never stand to doubt; Nothing's so hard, but search will find it out.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Dillon County Club '25
description: In the fall of '21 "Mac" left Dillon and came to Clemson to pursue that intangible thing called higher education. Here for four years he has been busy acquiring knowledge and friends—especially the latter, for "Mac" is one of those genial, happy-go-lucky fellows who make friends easily and keep them always. It is rumored that John Hugh is a member of a certain notorious club—but at his request the title is omitted. Everyone, however, knows that his weakness is for a certain form of plastic substance. "J.H." made the fatal mistake of becoming a member of "Tsukiyama's" class, but we predict that with his optimistic nature and native ability he will overcome this handicap before many years have elapsed. Well, "Mac" we, your friends, hate to part with you, for you have a place for yourself in our hearts, and we wish for you all good luck, and a long, happy future.

name: McGrewe, Clinton Jackson
motto: Honor Thy Father and Thy Mother That Thy Days May Be Long.
description: Falteringly, "Mac" left Sumter in the fall of 1920 and set his course towards the "Tiger" lair. It seems that he came to like the place, and the place to like him, for it clung to him an extra year. In the beginning "Mac" intended to enlist with the "Ags," but some little tilt of destiny made him cast his lot with the Mechanicals instead; and later with daring energy he took on both Mechanical and Electrical Engineering. "Mac" is not an athlete. But he has given a generous portion of his time to "Y" activities and another portion to certain other activities which have earned for him the cognomen "Dangerous Dan"—a term as endearing as it is significant. One with such a store of cheer must always share it with another; and so we prophesy he will soon share it with another. So here's to you, "Mac," may you always be happy and successful in your work.

name: Bauer, Jacob Willard
motto: To be of use in the world is the only way to be happy.
description: Sincerity and unselfishness soon won for a warm spot in the class of '25. We have ever found him a friend—a faithful and loyal friend, the four years that he has spent in our midst. came to Clemson with the sole idea of making a Civil Engineer out of himself. With this purpose in view he cast his lot with "Will Rogers" and proceeded to survey the countryside. By applying himself diligently has succeeded in accomplishing his purpose, and may truly call himself a Civil Engineer. Though kept his purpose in view, he did not let it interfere with other more pleasant duties, for he has been known to make week-end trips to see members of the "fairer sex." we feel that by your ability to persevere at whatever task there is to do, success cannot be denied you, and we hope to hear of you being at the top in your chosen profession in a short time. We shall watch you and will always cherish your remembrance. The best of luck to you always.

name: Bell, Samuel Lewis
motto: Truly Great is he, Who over self obtains mastery.
description: has been, throughout his college career, a friend of sterling qualities—cheerful, congenial, studious and, above all, manly. The Electrical world has held a strong appeal for this lad for a long period. It could not be more befitting than that he chase the "coulombs" with us. He is quiet and is a master of his own affairs. is ever ready to help a fellow out of trouble. He is the constructive type of person of whom any college would be proud.

name: McLees, Frank Calhoun
motto: I hate a thing done by halves. If it be right, do it wholly; if it be wrong, leave it undone.
clubs: Expert Machine Gunner, First Class Pistol Shot, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: He came to Clemson with one big idea; namely, to learn to produce more and better milk. He first set out to discover some law which would revolutionize the dairy industry, but soon became content to master the numerous laws already set forth by the leading dairy scientists. He has succeeded with his aim, and in so doing has accomplished many other things. His friends are numbered by his acquaintances. Many joyful hours have been spent by numerous friends listening to him impersonate his many profs and schoolmates, in a manner that only he is capable of doing. Judging from the many letters that Calhoun receives we feel sure that he has something on his mind besides the modern dairy, which is the ideal of his dream. With the good disposition which he possesses, the ability to make and to hold friends, and the determination to achieve the final goal, the greatest success will follow whatever course he may pursue.

name: McLeod, Neil Alexander
motto: A good word for a bad one is worth much and costs little.
clubs: Pistol and Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Neil, commonly known as "Dizzy", is a product of the lower country, hailing directly from Latta. We can only imagine what kind of boy he was at home, and in this imagination we can see nothing that speaks against the unblemished character of him. During his brief sojourn of four years with us, his work has been characterized by everlasting patience and self-sacrifice to the extreme. He has been a true Tiger and a loyal friend, always looking for some good turn to do, and never tiring of helping a friend in need. He is an active worker in the church and Sunday school, Y.M.C.A., and Bible Class—a real Christian of whom we are proud. Neil Alexander Paul McLeod, go forth to meet and to conquer the world with the best wishes and recommendations that your fellow classmen can bestow upon you—keep up your good work!

name: McLeod, Thomas Eugene
motto: In the state of mind.
clubs: Pistol Sharpshooter, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In the fall of '21, Eugene left the little town of Latta to seek an education, hence he landed in Tigertown. Since entering college he has been a leader in his class. He has always made very excellent marks in his studies. He is quiet and has very little to say, but means everything he does say. He is always willing to act the part of a friend or to lend a helping hand to anyone in need. We look to you to be a leader in social life after we depart from the hills of the Blue Ridge. May you always strive and stand out for our grand old Alma Mater.

name: McPhail, Miyauooto
motto: Determination makes dreams come true.
description: This is he. He came to Clemson in the fall of '21 and joined the ranks of extra walkers and post office visitors. He hails from a small town in the upper part of the state known as Townville. He fell in line with the Aggies when he first came and later specialized in 'Bugs.' It would be useless to try to enumerate all of the good characteristics of him, for if we did, it would take up the whole volume of Taps. A true Tiger, a fighter, a friend, and a gentleman—what more can be said of any man? As a pal, he can be counted on to stand by you to the last. He is a quiet easy going fellow choosing to hear rather than to be heard. His good nature and amiable disposition have won for him the love of every Clemson man, faculty members included. In some mysterious way, he has acquired the habit of shooting the professors; consequently, has become a regular 'E' man, never being satisfied with anything but the best.

name: Mace, Kenneth Murrell
motto: A man can bear a world's contempt when he has within, that which says he's worthy.
clubs: Secretary-Treasurer, Vice-President, President Marion County Club
Dairy Judging Team, National Dairy Show, '24
description: "Cap" entered "Tigertown" in the fall of 1920. After one year of special work, he decided to enter upon the regular curriculum, and with an undefeatable determination he has made an enviable record. However, there is always some weakness in every strong fellow. "Cap's" weakness seems to be with the ladies. It is rumored that he has fallen for one of the "fair damsels"—we do not know that it's true, but we do know that she will be a lucky girl if she gets "Cap." In the class room, on the campus, and on the athletic field he has made his mark. Now as each of us depart to the many walks of life, each member of the class carries with him a deep affection for you "Cap", and when you enter the state of matrimonial bliss, may your life be filled with something besides buildings.

name: Matthews, John DeLeon
motto: It's never too late to do good.
description: "Dad" steamed into the Tiger's lair in the fall of '15. Desiring to answer his country's call to arms, he deserted his classmates to join the khaki-clad boys in France, Belgium, Luxembourg, Germany and Italy. "Dad" says "war is hell," and he ought to know for he has five bronze stars on his service badge. After resisting the foreign types of the opposite sex, "Dad" finally fell for one of the variety grown in Florence County. After all the rice throwing and dodging old shoes, Clemson was again picked as a place of adventure. The class of '25 gladly welcomed "Dad" into their midst, and in spite of a continual fear of flunking (which never happened) we know that Civil Engineering circles will take on new life when he goes forth from Clemson with transit in his one hand and leading "Dad, Jr." at the rear end of a surveyor's chain. We sincerely admire him for again taking up his course after so long an interval and wish him many good things.

name: Maxwell, Robert Earle
motto: Don't let things slide; they might catch on a nail.
description: Right this side, Ladies and Gentlemen, is "Bob." Columbia claims him as her own—that may be true as far as the business side is concerned but not for all sides—for we know of a certain "sweet thing" elsewhere who claims him for all her own (He hopes). "Bob" is quite a philosopher. Sometimes, when he is meditating, we wonder whether something "Crip" tried to tell him or whether something "She" told him is about to predominate. We won't argue about that, though; for looking at both sides we are led to prophesy success in both electricity and—well, the happy side of life. We would not attempt to weigh his characteristics in such limited space, because even scales are not that accurate. "Bob" is a true friend, and as a companion he is all that can be desired. For him, we predict a future that is well worth looking forward to.

name: Melton, Lucius Hopkins
motto: You can't win the game on your own side of the scrimmage line.
description: Folks, we are now in the beauty section. You are gazing on no less personage than "Foote Street, Melton." "Doc," as he is familiarly known to every one on the campus, a few folks in York and a few folks in the Old North State came to Clemson in the fall of '21 from Chester High where he made an impressive record in three forms of athletics: football, baseball and track. He is the hard-hitting third baseman of the Tiger baseball team, who the Anderson College girls are always hoping will get a knock-out, and is quarterback on the Tiger football team. It can be truly said of him that he is a cadet, athlete, and gentleman of the highest type. In the last respect, "Doc" has been the victim of circumstances—"too much politics." We are looking to you, Melton, we know that you have the goods.

name: Miller, James Roberson
motto: Be sure you are right before going too far.
clubs: Member Live Stock Judging Team, Southeastern Fair
description: Yes, this is a loyal Tiger from York who has been with the class of '25 from "Rathood" to "Seniorhood." Be it said that York's loss was Clemson's gain. He is a capable animal husbandryman, and it is easy to see that he has his major work at heart. He proved his thorough knowledge of livestock by the high place which he won in the judging contest at the Southeastern Fair. His interest in, and loyal support of, all Tiger athletic teams has been very outstanding, and this loyalty has been surpassed only by the loyalty which he has shown towards his many friends. Once known, he is always liked because of his sunny disposition, pleasing personality, and sterling character. About the ladies? Oh yes! He is a prince of a good fellow, but among the "Queens" he is a "King." Here's to your future success and happiness. The pure-as-gold qualities of promptness, perseverance, and frankness which your classmates have found to be traits of your character never fail to win their way in the game of life.

name: Moore, Percy Whitton
motto: A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches.
description: He hails from Florence and he is proud of it. He came to Clemson to take up the study of electrical engineering, and it may be said that he has done credit to himself in that line. If he doesn't become a second Edison or Steinmetz it won't be his fault. He is liked by all who know him for he has all the qualities of a real fellow. He doesn't like the girls much, but he is mighty crazy about "the girl." He seems to have a chronic case of heart trouble, but it doesn't affect his health much as he has a mighty fine "doctor." Don't worry, for they all fall sooner or later. We are expecting great things of you and wish you the best of luck in your life work. Your many Clemson friends are counting on you.

name: Brown, Lewis Murr
motto: Do it now!
description: "Better late than never," must have rested hard upon Bill's mind, for "Bill", as he is known by all, waited several years after finishing Chester High before starting his last lap to knowledge. "Bill" came to Clemson with two ends in view. Electrical Engineering and the Art of Shooting "Bull," and surely if a man of the class of '25 achieved this success it is "Bill". "Bill" has won the high esteem and admiration of both the faculty and students, especially "Crip" because he is the only man since the class of '06 to thrill "Crip" in the least with his wide awake yarns. This handsome brute, with the assistance of a known fair dame of Chester, will surely be heard from in the future. "Bill" alone is a model for us all, and from what he says, we know that he and his fair lady from Chester match perfectly. Therefore nothing but success could possibly await Bill in the battle of life.

name: Norton, Joseph Jeptha Jr.
motto: A man's ability must not be measured by his efforts, but by his whole life.
description: Always friendly, always cheerful, Norton has carved a niche in the halls of Clemson and in the hearts of his friends that will stand in the days to come as a reminder of the one we knew as "Jo-Jo." Never seeking the limelight, but ever-ready to give his all for his school, he will be long remembered by those who knew him. When he was called upon to support any movement or any activity, he was always ready to give his time and his ability towards bringing the movement or activity to a successful end. Tireless in his efforts, willing in his attitude, "Jo-Jo" was ever among those who led the class. If he carries on in after life, there will be no other road open to him than the one which leads to success.

name: Patterson, Samuel Nathaniel
motto: Gather your kisses while ye may, time brings only sorrow; for the flappers who flap too freely today, are the chaperons of tomorrow.
description: Words fail us when we think of this man's career, we cannot say what he intends to do with his wonderful talent; however, we believe he will make a name for himself no matter what walk of life he pursues. "Pat," as we know him, is a fine youngster, he has proved this by the number of friends that he has made among his fellow students. It can be well said of "Pat" that he is a true friend to everyone who knows him. "Pat" is a handsome brute and with his always winning smile he is doubtless a lady-killer. "Pat" has often spoken of a bungalow just for two in some quiet place where he can have peace and happiness; if this be the case, the world can roll on and he will roll with it. "Pat," may success be yours in whatever you may choose.

name: Poe, Edward Jasper, Jr.
motto: Let me fail in trying to do something, rather than sit still and do nothing.
description: With but very few exceptions, Poe is ahead of the rest of us. He had been married one and a half years when he entered Clemson and is now the proud father of three daughters. Poe came to Clemson from Calhoun county, but he is a "Tar Heel" by birth and quite often we hear stories of his amazing experiences in the mountains where the "dew" is always present. He is liked by all his classmates and has many friends on the campus. The number of his friends always increases in the classroom when one of the professors gives us a quiz, because he seems to be the only one of us who can think to bring paper to class. He was a member of the Artillery Brigade of the Thirtieth Division during the World War. He spent nine months in France and England with the American Expeditionary Forces and was wounded September 12, 1918, near Toul, during the St. Mihiel drive.

name: Pope, Thomas Herbert
motto: It does not pay to worry unless you worry for pay.
description: Bang and away he goes. Tom, alias "Foxy Bill" alias "Toby" started off his military career with a rush. Under the masterful "Mat's" regime, "Toby" rose to the supreme heights of a First Sergeant. But alas; like Sampson, Napoleon, Oberg and other great military geniuses, our Auburn-haired hero fell from glory. 'Twas a great battle, Old Boy, and though you lost, you well deserve the title of "Colonel Toby." "Foxy Bill" has played good football with the scrubs, and to the scrubs half of the glory is due. With his figure and three inches more in height, we believe that he would have been a sensation in "ping-pong" circles. "Tom," Old Pal—Old Bud—Old Friend—Here's to your jovial spirit and your youthful figure, may you keep them both, and when you settle down by the fireside in after life we know that you will remember the friends you have made at Clemson.

name: Price, George Elvin
motto: Judge not a man by his face.
description: Here you see the boy with more nicknames than he has hair on his head. Though "Stoneface" is taking Electrical Engineering, he is more interested in sawmill machinery than in anything else. However, "Thirty-Inch" is also a military genius, having risen as high as sergeant in Clagett's army during his Junior year. The killing pace that he set for the unmilitary privates of that company was awful. In the mess hall "Ivory" was especially fond of biscuits. This earned him another nickname. Such things only go to show how popular a man is, though; and very few boys have as good a disposition and character as George. Here's to you, "Biscuit," may your face lead you safely through the world and some day stand out prominently in the Electrical World.

name: Blair, James William
motto: Be a friend to all, but love only one.
clubs: Secretary and President, Palmetto Literary Society
First Class Gunner, Howitzer Platoon, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: In such a short space of time as four years, we who have been associated with "Bill" have learned to appreciate the glow that has been shed upon us from his life. The sincerity and purity of his friendship have predominated our acquaintance with him in the most pleasing manner. When you, stranger, have come into contact with the glow of his friendship, you will have found one of life's most precious jewels, a true friend. "Bill's" entry into Clemson was not glaring nor spectacular, neither has his stay been spent in meteoric splendor, but where is the great man whose life was one great flare. The lives of such men are usually pleasantly forgotten. Whenever we may call to pay a visit in the future, "Bill," may we find that Life has smiled on you and yours most bountifully.

name: Blakeney, Lewis Boyd
motto: Every man has in himself a continent of undiscovered character. Happy is he who acts the Columbus of his own soul.
clubs: Secretary and President, Columbian Literary Society
Welter-weight Boxing Champion Fourth Corps Area
Rifle Marksman, Pistol Sharpshooter, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: A little girl's sky was beclouded while all Lancaster was somewhat enveloped in a veil of mourning, when "Blake" broke loose for Clemson. Lancaster's loss was Clemson's gain however, for he quickly made his presence felt here by his personality and thoughtfulness of others. He chose Electricity as his major study and to that end devoted his untiring energy. Anyone gazing upon the unscarred face of "Blake" would never dream of a pugilist. However, he demonstrated his ability in the manly art while at camp. In addition, "Blake" has fought like a true Tiger to do his bit for his Alma Mater on the track. His four years having been completed, his career, in our minds, is crowned with golden success, which few attain.

name: Roark, Robert Bright
motto: If you can't be good, be careful.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Pickens County Club '23
Rifle and Pistol Marksman, B.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: After having considerable experience in breaking up stills he decided to be an engineer and came to Clemson for his training. He cast his lot with the electricals, and was one of the first to solve "Crip's" switchboard puzzle. He is one of the very few who has never had a "D" or "F" written by his name during his stay at Clemson. A generous disposition, a sense of loyalty to his friends, and a strong determination to succeed have won for him a host of friends at Clemson. However, he has one great weakness; that is, he lets a "fair one" influence him to spend most every week-end in or near Pickens. We predict a brilliant future for him in the electrical world. Luck to you.

name: Robinson, Charles Alexander
motto: The measure of life is not length, but honesty.
clubs: Winner of Physical Efficiency Medal, Camp McClellan '24
description: Soon after he came to Clemson he won our love and respect, and admiration, with his individuality, sincerity and fineness of purpose. From the very beginning he has been recognized as a boy with great determination who has always stood up for what he thought best. Many are the times that the loyal Sons of Clemson cheered him on the cinder path, and more often, as he sprinted around Furman's ends and other football teams. As a football Captain, he would make some girl a good pilot for a home for two. The Class of '25 wishes you the greatest success that can be won in this world.

name: Roche, Thomas Gedding
motto: Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice; Take each man's censure, but reserve thy judgment.
description: "T.G.'s" one great weakness is the fairer sex. He never misses a chance to speak, and when he talks he usually gets results, because he possesses what is commonly known as a "good line." They all fall sooner or later, and when the masculine person in the entanglement happens to be Geddings the time is always sooner never later.

name: Rhodes, Carlton Fuller
motto: Genius wins sometimes, hard work always.
description: Carlton hailed from Darlington County, and not from "Rhodesia" as his name might infer. He came to Clemson in the fall of '21 after seventeen years of practical experience on the farm. He chose to discontinue his previous work and elected to specialize in electricity. When once your friend, always your friend. His pleasing personality has won for him many friends. No task seems too large for Carlton. Early in his pursuit of study, he displayed wonderful ability; and has always stood high in his class work. Cupid has constantly directed his arrows at him; but as yet there is no external sign of injury. We feel that the fatal shot will be fired soon. With the determination that he possesses, we can see nothing between him and success.

name: Rush, James Floyd
motto: Give to the world the best you have and the best will come back to you.
