description: Rush, after being out of school for eight years, decided to obtain a better knowledge of agriculture. In the fall of '21 he came to Clemson with the determination to prepare himself for the teaching of agriculture, and with such a determination has made Tsukiyama a noble disciple. In this respect he may be considered fortunate because he can now boast of having a future Clemson football star who answers to the name of "Billy" Rush. We wish "Daddy Rush" all the success in the world in his endeavor to teach the boys how to grow cotton and sell it to the best advantages.

name: Salley, Henry Dempsey
motto: Lift up the man who is down, don't kick him out.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Aiken County Club '23
Machine Gun Expert, Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This promising young man of the class of '25 is universally known as "Goat." It has been said that a "red headed" man makes a success of everything he undertakes; it is truly so with "Goat," for he seems to possess the knack of "shooting" all of his professors. With his good nature and winning smile he has made a close friend of every Clemson man. "Goat" is a man who is loyal and true, and whose principles are an inspiration to all who know him. His big heart and sympathetic nature have won for him the friendship and respect of the entire class, who unite in wishing him the best that life can give.

name: Sanders, Edward Jr.
motto: Take pride in keeping good company and good hours.
clubs: Second Lieutenant, King Cole's and Horse's Regiment
description: Look out! Here comes as the ladies call him, but only to the boys. This chap came to Clemson from Okatee, S. C., the land of palmettoes (ask the rats). has always been a hard working fellow, and the army often caught him burning "Midnight" oil studying for a quiz for some of the wiser heads. Last year was often consulted by his classmates for information on "Dickie's" immortal quizzes. has always proved himself a clean, upright, manly sport, and has always stood for what he thought was right. This lad has always been quite popular with the ladies; the postmaster can testify to that truth. If the symptoms now present continue to develop, he will sit at the head of a large table somewhere in this state, we hope and believe.

name: Sanders, Kenneth Black
motto: To earn my keep—if I may, And if I may—to cheer some gloomy brother; . . . . . That is all.
clubs: Editor, Clippings and Comments Department of Chronicle
description: made his debut at Clemson in the fall of '21 as a trembling freshman from Colleton County, and ever since then he has been at work making a place for himself in college circles. is an earnest worker, always doing his best in everything that he tackles. This trait has made him known both in class work and in his work on the Chronicle staff where his pen has been a great support to the magazine. There is one little flaw in this chemist's make-up—chemistry is his only love! He is one of the few real genuine woman-haters in captivity. We, his friends, predict, however, that he will eventually succumb to the wiles of some member of the fairer sex. Well, old fellow, your frankness, friendliness, and trustworthiness have made you a friend from whom it is hard to part; but, since we must part, we wish for you a happy, successful future.

name: Sanftleben, Daniel Adolph
motto: I'll not confer with sorrow Till to-morrow; But joy shall have her way This very day.
description: This tall handsome lad came from the far frozen north. New York unfortunately lost "Dan" in the fall of '21. He knew very few boys when he arrived at Clemson, but with his winning personality and genteel disposition he made friends everywhere. As a course, "Dan" chose Civil Engineering, and judging from the love he shows for his work we are sure he must have chosen the right course. He has all the "symptoms" of a successful engineer, but we fear he has one weak spot—a pretty brunette could make him forget whether he took Agronomy or Engineering. At all times "Dan" was an ardent supporter of student activities. He was on the tennis team for four years, and during his Senior year was Captain of the team. He was one of the best tennis players in the state during his Junior and Senior years. In his studies he was an earnest worker, and we feel sure that he will make a great engineer in the years to come. We wish you luck, "Dan."

name: Seago, John Alvin
motto: Respect yourself and avoid even the appearance of evil.
clubs: Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
First Lieutenant of "Highpocket" Hambright's Gang of Major "H"—"B.J." "M.B." Darby's Battalion
description: 'Twas a bright afternoon in the fall of '21 that Greenwood suffered a very serious loss, when "Bessie" boarded the "Cannon Ball Express" for Tigertown. In his classes, "Bessie" has always been an "E" man, and as a friend, he has been always ready and willing to help a fellow out. Even the little rats have taken a liking to him, and love to see him get mad and break off chair rounds (for their pleasure?) This is a lad whom everyone can trust, regardless of his looks. Even "Sam" has confidence in him to let him be "Light fixer" in barracks. When camp was over, not even 'ole sunny Alabam' could keep him from the lowlands of Mississippi. Somewhere out there he tells us he has a sweetheart—he may have, but there are a few "Doubting Thomases" among our crew. Here's to you, "Bessie," luck, success, and happiness in that "air bungalow" you are always raving about.

name: Shanklin, Julius Augustus
motto: "On my tomb, if ever I have one, I mean to get these words inscribed: 'He clung to his paddle.'"
clubs: Secretary and Vice-President of Greenville County Club
description: The time for mourning at Greer had come in the fall of 1921, when Julius Shanklin bade his playmates and friends a fond farewell to pursue a four-year course at Clemson; however, the mourning will be more than compensated by rejoicing when Julius returns in the Spring of 1925, with his degree in his hand and his head full of knowledge. Julius had the advantage over almost all of his classmates by having decided what course he wanted at college. His choice was Horticulture and he has been diligently striving to reach the goal for four long years. Evidently, Julius has had many a sweetheart because he is well versed in the art of smiling and is always having something pleasant to say. Never did he have military aspirations; consequently, he did not make a punctual soldier, but he has more than made up for this by making a host of friends during his college career.

name: Sharp, James Marshal
motto: "If I cannot do great things, I can do small things in a great way."
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Yes, this lad is a South Carolinian, but it is hard to find his habitat on any map. He came to Clemson in the fall of '21 in search of an education. After having searched the entire curriculum "Sticker" decided that he would make some improvements in the textile industry of the South, and therefore, cast his lot with the "Swine-nites" with the sole desire of becoming a Textile Engineer. "Sticker" is truly worthy of his nickname, for he has pursued his studies with that "never-say-die" spirit throughout his four years' stay at Clemson. Success is assured to a man of his determination and stickability. Jim has a smile and a word of cheer for everyone, and is as sincere as the best of them. Although this lad professed to be "Woman Proof" during his first years at Clemson, it is rumored that Love has taken charge—a fair maiden has captured his heart. We wish you the best that the world can offer a consistent fighter. We can vouch for your success.

name: Shealy, Noah Patrick
motto: Success lies, not so much in holding a good hand, but in playing a poor hand well.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Newberry County Club '21
Third Highest Expert Machine Gunner, Pistol Marksman, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: He came to Clemson in the fall of '21 determined to be an architect and cast his lot with the beauty worshippers. He is the kind of a fellow that says little, but thinks much. He has demonstrated his determination to be an architect by his untiring efforts in performing the tasks that have been assigned to him. If the old adage be true that "Success is one-tenth inspiration and nine-tenths perspiration," then nothing less than success can come to this lad in his chosen vocation. The frankness and sincerity that mark men of character are both characteristic of this lad. With his unassuming ways, and thoughtfulness of everyone, together with his cheerfulness and sincerity has won many friends during his stay at Clemson. He has not only made friends of his fellow-students, but has also won many girl friends. May luck be with you always!

name: Simpson, William Nathaniel
motto: Believe every man to be honest unless you are sure he is otherwise.
clubs: Howitzer Company, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Nat has made a wonderful record as a test-tube juggler, and is one of Dickey's most loyal disciples. By his smiling countenance, has won many friends during his four years at Clemson. If he continues in the future as he has done in the past he will be a great asset to the chemical world. Though famous in the chemical world, is not known in the military world. If there's anything he dislikes it's "King Cole's Army." He began his dislike for wearers of "boots and spurs" early in his "rat" year and has not outgrown this dislike.

name: Smith, Drayton Edgeworth
motto: A little said and truly said Can deeper joy impart Than hosts of words that reach the head But never reach the heart.
clubs: "Dirty Dozen Dead-Beats," R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Drayton came to Clemson with the sole idea of making a horticulturalist of himself; and we can truthfully say that he has succeeded in building a foundation that is capable of withstanding the hard knocks of life. He would lead us to believe that the ladies play a small part in his life and under the tutelage of his "old lady," Paul Stoney, we anticipate nothing less than a bachelor's life for him. We feel sure that with the good disposition, the ability to make and hold friends, and the determination to achieve the final goal, the greatest success will follow whatever course he may pursue. If you are as successful as your ambitions warrant, "D.E.", we can certainly envy you in the business world. Here's to you, Drayton!

name: Smith, Earle Lewis
motto: Live your life as you see best Play the game, fate will do the rest.
clubs: Member of Team Holding American Record for the Distance Medley Relay
Member of Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: E. Lewis, star representative of the Electric City, is a man of so many varied activities that it is hard to decide which side of his checkered career to chronicle here. Athlete? Yes, Track Captain, not to mention football and basketball. Scholar? Surely (ask the professor of education). Orator? He speaks with distinction in the state contest. Editor? Look at the Tiger. But in spite of his stellar performances in all of these roles, if we were casting E. Lewis for his biggest show, we would put him in the character of a heart-smasher. Here he leads the field. His notable record extends from his native heath in Anderson to the confines of Winthrop and Converse, and to many other places.

name: Blakeney, Lee Roy
motto: Be what you are.
clubs: Winner of Medal for Highest Machine Gun Score at Camp McClellan, R.O.T.C., Fourth Corps Area '24
description: Gifted with a genial personality, has made for himself a reputation since coming to Clemson that will stand in the hearts of those who knew him long after college days are over. Always eager to help his friends, always ready to lend his support to any enterprise that would advance his school, is the kind of man that we like to call a true son of Clemson. While not a finished scholar, he has ever been able to stand above the line that bounds success and failure in class-room work. In student activities he has been faithful and earnest, never failing to fulfill the part he was given to perform. May his success in after life be as brilliant as that which he has attained sojourning at Tigertown.

name: Bonner, Thomas Andrew
motto: Perfection is not found in man. Though educated well; To do just right he never can, Because poor Adam fell.
clubs: Columbian Literary Society, Junior Critic '24, Treasurer '25
description: To know is to be acquainted with a good fellow and a true gentleman. A big heart, a generous disposition, coupled with hard work and sincerity won for many friends while at Clemson, and the same attributes will carry him to success in life. came to Clemson in the fall of '21 and immediately cast his lot with "Crip," and ever since he has pursued the elusive electron with determination and zeal. Some day we hope to hear of some great work he has performed in the electrical world. For being able to "skin" reveille without being caught by the "Army," became the envy of "Pecks Boys," who had to smother a yawn, grab a shirt, a pair of trousers, and push out in the cold to answer while buttoning up.

name: Spencer, Bert Monroe
motto: Determination is the master key to success.
clubs: Expert Machine Gunner, Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Geographically speaking, Bert's "briar patch" is Madison, S. C., the first city this side of the great divide between Georgia and the Old South State. In September of 1921 the people of this thriving metropolis did not realize that the lad who was leaving for Clemson would some day be one of the greatest architects of this era. Neither did they realize that some day this chap would be a "shave-tail" in King Cole's Army, nevertheless the old home town has been awakened to these surprising facts. Bert is sure to make good wherever he goes because of his winning personality, his cheerful smile and everlasting determination to complete whatever he takes in hand to do. Sometime in the dim future this noble gentleman will return to the old home town to spend his fortune and old age in the hills of western South Carolina.

name: Smith, Richard Hallum
motto: One day with life and heart, is more than time enough to find a world.
clubs: Junior Literary Critic and Corresponding Secretary Columbian Literary Society '24
Secretary and Treasurer Colleton County Club '24
Pistol Sharpshooter and Rifle Marksman, Camp McClellan '24
description: Smoaks claims him as her own; any place would be proud to call him hers—this lad, whom we all know as "R.H." He has won the friendship and admiration of all who know him. "Dick" is the embodiment of congeniality, sincerity, and manhood. He is quick to make friends and he keeps them. "R.H." came to Clemson in the fall of '21, and here the following of his ideals has carried him into many phases of college life. Through all, however, his trusty pen has stood him in good stead, for Smith has been one of the leading figures in the literary field of the class of '25. As "ye olde editor," "poet," Smith has no equal.

name: Stewart, Edwin Clarke
motto: One lone crowded hour of glorious life is worth a whole world without a name.
clubs: Delegate Student Volunteer Convention at Indianapolis, Ind.
description: Possessed with a pleasing personality and the ability to make friends, has created a bond of companionship with all who knew him in his four-year sojourn at Clemson. Always willing to lend his efforts toward the advancement of his school; always ready to carry out his part of any activity, is the kind of man we like to call a true son of Clemson. While not a brilliant athlete or the brightest scholar in his class, he has shown determination that leads to the top. If he carries on in after life with the same spirit and earnestness, there will be no highway before him except the straight road to success.

name: Stewart, James Daniel Jr.
motto: A good friend is better than great riches, and a square meal is better than fame.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Florence County Club '23
Honor Student and Gamma Tau Delta of Georgia Tech '21
description: Having spent his Freshman year at Georgia Tech, joined the class of '25 as a Sophomore. During his three years at Clemson, has, in every way, been an asset to his class. Especially on the athletic field and in the class-room does he rank with the best. His proficiency as an athlete and as a student is due not alone to natural ability but to natural ability coupled with a willingness to work and a desire to make good. To here tell all of his good qualities is an impossibility; let me add that as a friend—well, one never had and never could find a finer and truer friend than. We wish you the best of luck; give to the world as you have to Clemson and success cannot be but yours.

name: Stevens, John Townsend
motto: "The mind has a thousand eyes, the heart but one. Yet the light of the whole life dies when love is done; so think before you make a promise, then stick to it."
clubs: Pistol and Rifle Marksman, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Here he is, Steve, himself. This lad descended from the Wild Cat den at Davidson, but is as true a Tiger as ever inhabited the Tiger den. As a bugler he is quite the stuff and has won distinction as leader of the famous Drum-and-Bugle Corps, besides making the Band a good Drum Major. His frequent excursions to the Hotel bodes much evil for the state of bachelorhood and we predict soon the second great period in a man's life (according to Dr. Daniel.) John is a true friend and an earnest worker in his chosen profession of Horticulture. We know that he will succeed and we wish him all the luck and happiness in the years to come.

name: Stokes, Kenneth Erwin
motto: "Nothing is so congenial as a clear conscience."
clubs: Sophomore, Junior, and Senior Dancing Clubs
Sharpshooter Pistol and Machine Gun R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Kenneth came to Clemson in the fall of '21 and announced himself as a candidate for a B. S. degree along with a number of other "rats" who were afraid of what their fate would be at each minute. He is efficient in the class-room and also as a supporter of Tiger teams and is always ready to lend a helping hand in all the student activities. Yes, he is a gentleman, a true friend, and a true Tiger, and we do not hesitate to say that the elements that have prompted him to the place that he holds in the hearts of his friends will surely, without a doubt, push him up to the last step of the ladder of success. Kenneth, as you leave us, just remember that we hold you in our hearts as one we shall always count as a true friend, and we wish you and yours the best of everything.

name: Stoney, Paul DeWees
motto: He could on either side dispute, confute, change hands, and still confute.
description: Gangway, here he comes! P.D. became tired of doing nothing but follow the slogan "Watch Allendale grow," so he came to Clemson in search of knowledge. Judging from his flow of sesquipedalian words in writing and talking, we feel sure that his search is over. Now, fellows, if you ever lose your dictionary while with P.D., don't worry, you have a walking encyclopedia with you. We, of America, when looking for new styles, turn our eyes toward Paris. But why cross the briny deep, boys, when we have with us one who far excels the Parisians in his selections? "As Stoney goes so go the styles." To quote his own words—"I am not so good looking, but I do wear cute clothes." P.D. chose horticulture. In this he has grafted, budded, and bloomed. Go to it, ole boy, we are wishing you a successful and happy future with the choice of your heart—L.A.

name: Stribling, David Warren
motto: Do you love life? Then do not squander time, for it is the substance of which life is made.
clubs: President and Secretary, Columbian Literary Society
Member Livestock Judging Team Southeastern Fair, Atlanta, Georgia '24
First Class Machine Gunner and Pistol Marksman, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: This young lad came to us from Westminster in the fall of '21. Westminster's loss was our gain, and ever since his arrival here he has been a popular member of the class of '25. Judging from the number of letters he gets he is also popular in other places. However, he does not let his social affairs interfere with his studies. His interest in student activities and his ability in class work have won for him the esteem and friendship of all who know him. "Strib" has chosen for his line of work the animal industry. He has already shown his ability as a livestock judge by the record he made at the Southeastern Fair. He plans to have a home of his own surrounded by fields and pastures, dotted with pure-bred cattle and hogs. We expect great things from this classmate of ours, and we wish him the best of luck in his chosen work.

name: Sutherland, John Lawrence
motto: The world doesn't ask what have you done, but what can you do.
clubs: Prosecuting Critic, Treasurer, Palmetto Literary Society
Machine Gun Expert, Pistol Marksman R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Lawrence hails from the dizzy heights of the scenic Blue Ridge. Probably we should say Pickens, but we know too well that his home is far enough above there to boast of a gorgeous view of Mount Pinnacle, and the gigantic Table Rock. After many years of glorious mountain adventures, awakened to the fact that this was not all in life, and that his future destiny lay at stake. Thus, it happened Clemson fell to his choice, and in the fall of '20 he answered the call. Lawrence was a wily freshie, a merry soph, a solemn junior and a determined senior. He has been a friend to all, a loyal Tiger and a hard worker. We find, too, he pulls a wicked trigger, and he has a keen sight for girls. Here's to you Lawrence, may the past, so well spent, be only a glimmer of what Dame Future may bestow.

name: Taber, William Preston
motto: The world doesn't ask what have you done, but what can you do.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Club
description: In the fall of '21 there came into our midst a tall lad from Fort Motte, seeking desperately for an education. He cast his lot with the engineers, and some day we expect to see him niche his name in the engineering hall of fame. Immediately after arriving at Clemson became very enthusiastic over military life, and all during his four years he has been a favorite of the wearers of the Sam Browne. With his training at B.M.I. and Clemson, he will be of unlimited service to Uncle Sam if he should ever need him. Is somewhat of a ladies' man. He admits he hasn't a very good "line", but we expect him to hook one before long. Here's to you, Taber, as you set sail on the sea of time, and may you be the friend in after life that you have been in your college days.

name: Talbert, Eddie Holmes
motto: "All things are good for me to use properly, decently, in moderation, and under the proper circumstances; but nothing is good enough to use me, or to master me."
clubs: R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala., Rifle Marksman, Pistol Marksman
description: "Eddie" decided to leave the historic town of Edgefield in the fall of 1921, to cast his lot in "Tigertown" for four years. If ever he had troubles no one knew it, because he seemed to be an artist at concealing his feelings. Throughout his college career he has followed the policy of leaving those whom he disliked alone, and sticking to those he liked under all circumstances; and being always ready to accept an apology or to give one, if he deemed it right. "Eddie" is a sincere friend, a good loser, and a perfect gentleman. The above qualities added to his generosity and pleasing personality have gained him a host of friends at Clemson who wish him well in whatever he undertakes.

name: Tate, Harold Simmons
motto: "God help me to be brave and strong, harboring no shameful thought, ready for the day's work."
clubs: Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: While at Clemson, Harold always impressed the ones with whom he came in contact with his friendliness, his good humor, and generosity. Under his breast there beats a heart that is faithful to the last. Harold has never striven for great honors, but was never found wanting when there was a need to be performed, and he has persevered at all times to his utmost. For four years Harold helped to mold a football team, serving unsung and unpraised on the scrubs, giving all his fight and courage that his Alma Mater might be well represented. Harold was an earnest student, and although obstacles were thrown in his pathway by the faculty in the forms of examinations, by his perseverance and determination he has reached the goal for which he strove. We feel sure that Harold's fighting qualities will carry him through life with great success.

name: Tennant, Allen Boyd
motto: God and the world loves a fighter—not the kind that will bravely lie down and die, but the kind that will fight like hell to live.
clubs: R.O.T.C., Howitzer Company, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Dutch," as this big-hearted lad is affectionately known, cast a shadow of gloom over the whole of Fairfield County including Winnsboro when he announced his determination to come to Clemson in the fall of '21 to become a football player and "bobbin dodger." He has made a number of friends in both departments, and "Swine" can truly boast that he is one man who is worthy to serve as a model for all of the others. Dutch, who is often referred to as the most dependable, most consistent man on Clemson's football team, will no doubt achieve the same honors in the textile industry in a very short time. He tackles jobs as he tackles hearts with an irresistible force. We bow to you, "Dutch," may your ambition be ours.

name: Thompson, Earl Alden
motto: If the game is going against you, keep your head erect, set your jaw, and dig in. This is what tests the stuff you are made of.
description: "Alabama," as we all know him, first came into our midst in the fall of '19. After remaining in school for three years, he decided that he wanted to see the world before he finished; so he left us then. It was not until the fall of '24 that we saw "Alabama" again. He came back to take up that old friendship, for it is true that if you ever know him, he is a true friend. This is one thing that he has proved to his fellow students. It can be said of "Alabama," that he has a stalwart character and he means to do nothing but succeed, and conquer the greater things in life. Whatever problem confronts him, we believe that he can down it like a man. Here's to you, "Alabama," that you may succeed in whatever you may choose, for we predict that with your ability, and self-reliance you will achieve great success in life.

name: Thrower, John Harrington
motto: A man is judged not only by what he does, but also by what he does not do.
description: Hip! Hip! Hurrah! for the boy from Cheraw. He neither drinks nor chews, nor will he sleep in any— is a big man from a little town. He came to Clemson with the ambition of becoming a senior "lint-head" and by dint of his long arms, he has reached his goal. chief sports are tennis and sleeping. In tennis, he has a faint resemblance to Tilden; and in sleeping, he is in the same bed with old "Rip"—the only difference is that has never gotten out of bed yet. expects to carry New York by storm, and we, his friends, realizing his many talents and his stunning personality, know that some day he will be a big man in a big town.

name: Tibbs, Ralph Homer
motto: If you can't say a good word for your fellow man, do not speak at all.
description: To be a friend of Ralph's is, indeed, to be a fortunate person, for no other friend could anyone desire. His magnetic personality, versatility, and determination to successfully accomplish what he starts has made for a record of which he may be justly proud. When the proverbial "Who's Who" of future engineers is published his name will surely be contained therein. Ralph has never claimed to be a ladies' man, but those that have observed his frequent trips to the P.O. know that several young ladies must have a busy time keeping his daily letter supply. In football and baseball has "done his stuff" with his characteristic energy—always optimistic, always pushing ahead. And as an entertainer—well just ask any Clemson Cadet. Warning: Don't get him started if time is scarce. Because of lack of space his most harrowing experience cannot be related here. May life hold many pleasant things for you, Ralph.

name: Todd, John Newton
motto: Life depends on how you live it.
clubs: Expert Machine Gunner, Co. D, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala., 1924
description: John packed up and journeyed to Tigertown because there is a time-honored tradition in Walhalla that every boy must go to Clemson. Early in his career at Clemson, John decided to expose himself to Entomology, so he became one of Reed's disciples. In order to become a full-fledged "bug" man he pursued Anthonomus grandis during his summer of 1923. John is a good, steady, hard-working student and stands high in his classes. Here's to you John, old pal, if you succeed in life like you do in the mess-hall you need not worry, for the future is yours.

name: Traxler, William Carroll
motto: Be not satisfied with the smaller things of life; strive for greater ones.
description: "Trax," as we all know him, came to us from the metropolis of Bowman in the fall of '21. Realizing the need of more and better educators in South Carolina, "Trax" decided to choose education as his major study, and to do his part in driving illiteracy from our state. He believes that success can be obtained only by sticking to each task that is undertaken until it is mastered. We can very readily see this from the success he has had in his college work. "Trax" is a real friend and one that can always be depended upon. He will always be among our most pleasant memories. We know very little of his love affairs, but we believe that he has some fair maiden who is just waiting for him to finish college and to build a little bungalow for two. With such excellent traits as originality, keen judgment, whole-heartedness, sociability, and nobleness of character, we can predict for him only success.

name: Welch, William Fletcher
motto: "Oh love, if death be sweeter, let me die."
clubs: National Dairy Show and World's Dairy Congress, Milwaukee, Wis., 1924
description: Small of stature, large of heart and head, witty, easy to fall in love, and with an ambition to own the dairy that feeds the world—these are some of the traits of "Wop." If "Wop" impresses the world as he has "Big Ben" and "J.P.", we expect to see him some day at the acme of his fame startling the Dairy world with "Welch Products." "Wop" is endowed with a personality that even the ladies cannot resist. It is rumored that he and "Sug" may succumb to the darts of "Dan Cupid" shortly after graduation. Few girls will be as fortunate as the one who captures the heart of "Wop." We are not prone to prophecy, but if such may be allowed in this case, we look into the future and see a happy, prosperous leader of the Dairy Industry, married of course, making life more pleasant for himself and others—this being none other than our midget classmate "Wop."

name: Welling, Charles Eugene, Jr.
motto: "Build for character, not for fame."
description: "Gene" arrived at Clemson in the fall of 1921 from the "City-by-the-Sea," and in about two weeks decided that "Father was Right"—that Charleston wasn't such a bad place after all. However, he has settled down now and hates to leave Clemson because of his love of that great mysterious thing known as Electricity, that he is studying about. "Gene" hopes to add a good definition of this word to Mister Webster's dictionary, some day. This lad is also an ardent student of Military Science—and he can certainly drill a company. However, "Gene" treats everyone fairly and is well-liked by all of his associates. He has a stern, frank disposition and says exactly what he means. These things coupled with his many other qualities, are bound to bring him success some day. Here's to you "Gene" just keep up the good work and time will bring results.

name: Williamson, John G.
motto: Smile and the world smiles with you—Life is only what we make it.
description: Conceive of a South Carolinian who goes across to North Carolina every day for his mail and there you have the above named. John G. came to us from the Independent Republic of Horry in the fall of 1921 and registered with the Ag's. As his life's work began to crystallize, he specialized in plants at the beginning of his junior year and concentrated his energy on agronomy in his final year at Clemson. As any man who has been at Clemson in recent years will know, John G. is a disciple of the "Lord"—who knows corn from the peak of its tassel to the tip of every root hair—and who also knows the history of every variety of cotton south of the North Pole. Yes, under the faithful teachings of Dr. Collings, John G. has learned many—yes many things "even as you and I." His ambition after leaving Clemson is to enlist in the army service in the rising crusade against our present farming conditions. He has a vision of fertile and productive areas in every portion of our state—and we hope to see his vision come true.

name: Wise, George Cutts
motto: A square deal for every man.
description: Stop, take a look at this man who is a man, and who could say more of anybody than, "He is a man?!" Although the city of Prosperity did not go to ruin when Cutts left to come to us in the fall of '21, we feel sure that the little city was somewhat lonesome for Cutts. But now, after this lad has struggled four years with the thing we call college life, he can once more go back and make his home town worthy of its name. He can do it. We expect great things from Cutts. We do not say that Cutts made the best grades in his class work; we do not even say he was the most brilliant man in the class of '25, but we do say that he knows no such a word as "can't". This man is a friend to all, and with this personality he cannot fail to succeed.

name: Wilson, Gary Cortez
motto: Never give up until you have attained that which seems impossible.
description: Behold this sorrel top lad who hails from the metropolis of Honea Path! The four years that "Red" spent in our midst were filled with activity, for he was one of the stalwart men on the football team that made the Tiger's line famous. "Red" not only worked on the gridiron, but also proved to be a consistent student and never failed to make his presence felt in the classroom. By his cheerfulness and willingness, "Red" earned for himself many friends in the student body. This is attested to by his great popularity. "Red", we know you as a true and dependable friend who will not fail to do the best at all times. If you tackle the battles of life as you tackled the foes on the gridiron, you have nothing to fear. The class of '25 knows you, "Red", as an athlete, a fighter, and a big-hearted chap who is bound to succeed in life. The best of luck to you!

name: Woodside, Harry Robert
motto: 'Tis better to have people wonder what he would have said than to wonder why he said it.
description: In the fall of '21, "Woody" as he is known in Tigertown, came to Clemson and cast his lot with the architects. During his stay here he has made many warm friends. Always willing to help some fellow student and never thinking of himself is very characteristic of "Woody." As to architecture, well he is some architect, and at present he is completing plans for a greater New York. Go to it, "Woody" 'ole boy, with your ability as an architect and that wonderful girl of yours, you can design buildings that will surpass the Chicago Tribune Building in beauty and splendor. We believe in you, "Woody," and we know that you can deliver the goods. In the near future the 'ole Palmetto State will be proud to call you one of her sons.

name: Youmans, Maxwell
motto: To live in hearts we leave behind is not to die.
description: The town of Fairfax lost one of her finest young men when he left his home in the fall of 1921 to cast his lot with the Dairy Maids of "Tiger-Town." When he reached "Tiger-town" he was a lad about five feet tall weighing about ninety pounds, but after four years of hard work he has reached the height of five feet nine inches and one hundred and fifty pounds. He has never had an easy time with his work, but has always been successful in accomplishing everything he has undertaken. He is one of few words, but of strong actions, and during his stay at Clemson he has made many friends among the professors and cadets, as well as among the "fairer-sex" of the campus. Although he does not talk much, we know he is dreaming of the future. Something worth while is all he would plan; and here's to you, that the future may hold rich rewards for you.

name: Popus, Red Hotus
motto: Don't let your toe-nail slip, it might cut the sheet.
description: Here he is, ladies! The cream of the corps—ice cream, cold cream, and whipped cream. He came to Jungletown one bright September morning after the freight on which he was riding had a hot-box at Calhoun. We are indeed proud of such a mythical genius of nature. Four long years he has accomplished heaps and done nothing. He is to be admired for his rheumatic personality; his taking ways have caused him to be quite popular among his fellow students—in fact, he takes everything before him. He is a conglomeration of ability. His elegance in speaking is only exceeded by that of "D.W."; his ability as a military genius is well known, and only Col. "Horse" Mullins can outwit him in his ability to use the arms. He has the appetite of Finklen, the language of Captain Higgins, and the moccasin step of Captain Lee. You may think this all a lie—well, it is.

name: Buck, Fred Edward
motto: A tiger born, a tiger bred, when I die—a tiger dead!
clubs: Senior Reception Committee for Athletic Teams
description: From early boyhood Buck has dreamed of being a military officer. In his high school days he planned to go to the Citadel, but in the fall of '21 he decided to cast his lot with the Clemson Tigers. No one knows where Buck acquired his military inclinations, but since his landing at Tigertown, the fighting blood of his forefathers has pushed him forward until now he holds the rank of Lieutenant Colonel in the Cadet Corps—and a popular Colonel is he. Buck is a jack-of-all-trades and is very good at all of them. He has done well in every phase of college activities at Clemson. He is an outstanding member of the Glee Club and the College Quartet. Buck has been on three state champion cross-country teams, and has taken an active part in literary societies and Y.M.C.A. work.

name: Bunch, Robert Lemuel
motto: If a task is once begun, never leave it till it's done; Be the labor great or small do it well or not at all.
clubs: Winner of R. W. Simpson Medal '24
description: Bob, commonly known as "Funny" to his fellow students, is another one of those men from the city-by-the-sea accustomed to the water around the "Battery"; he decided to follow the current when he came to Clemson. As a chaser of molecules "Funny" is right there with the goods. "Funny" is full of wit and always meets his fellow students with a smile. The name "Funny" was given him because of the number of bright remarks he is always pulling. Bob is one of the Captains in King Cole's army, and is one of the outstanding men of the regiment. He is not only a military man, but is one of the stars of his section. This is shown by the number of men who come to him for his opinions on the solution of some of Crip's problems and experiments.

name: Cannon, Edgar Allen
motto: If you want to, you can.
clubs: Gold McGat Howitzer Gunner Fourth Corps Area, R.O.T.C. Camp McClellan
description: Known to every Clemson Tiger as the boy from Blythewood, "Ed" has a place of fond friendship in every Tiger's heart. "Ed" has stood out, head and shoulders, above his classmates in class work, and in the activities of the college. Any person who has the wonderful personality and brilliancy of this young man will never stop with the mere principles of his life, but will strive to seek what is far beyond the surface of the work that he undertakes to do. Some day we classmates will hear about the Seventh wonder of the Engineering world, namely Edgar A. Cannon. We, the class of '25, will look to your success and may it be the best that can be obtained.

name: Cartee, Eugene Franklin
motto: Better do than dream; better be than seem.
clubs: Machine Gun Company, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Whole hearted and true hearted. What more could be said of anyone? The four years that Eugene spent in our midst were years of friendship, and we have ever found him true to the principles of life. Eugene hails from Liberty and that little town can well be proud of this son who upheld so nobly the fighting Tiger spirit. For three years Eugene was a star performer on the track team and never failed to give his best to bring victory to his Alma Mater. From the fact that Eugene goes off on week-ends to see one of the "fairer sex" and from the numerous letters he receives written in a feminine hand, we rather suspect that he might soon be tempted to embark upon the matrimonial seas. Eugene, your friends of the class of '25 are confident that with your personality, intelligence, and well known ability to work, success can not be denied you. Here's wishing you luck always.

name: Craven, William Herbert
motto: "He who lives without folly is not so wise as he thinks."
clubs: Recording Secretary, Wade Hampton Literary Society
description: This hearty lad entered Clemson in the fall of '21 with the determination of acquiring an education. He has kept the faith, fought a good fight, and won the victory. Herbert is a good worker and does not believe in putting off until tomorrow that which can be done today. While here, Herbert has won a host of friends. To know him is to like him. He is always ready to help those who are in trouble. We will always hold a warm place in our hearts for him. He is also quite popular with the ladies. The girl who is fortunate enough to win this young man will be fortunate indeed, for she will have not only a real man, but one with a big heart. With his strong determination, keen ability, winning personality, and generous disposition, we can predict nothing but success for him in the future.

name: Daniel, Derrill McCollough
motto: "Love many, trust few, but always paddle your own canoe."
clubs: Junior Literary Critic, Senior Literary Critic, Horticulture Society
"K.P." Club, Company B, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "Bebe," as this fair lad is known to his classmates, came rolling into Tigertown on a beautiful September morn in the fall of '21 with the sole purpose of preparing himself for his future life. "Bebe" hails from Cooper and we believe that Cooper suffered a severe loss when he left, but her loss was our gain. Early in his college career "Bebe" decided that the lure of "bug" chasing was too strong to resist; so he became one of Reed's disciples and joined "Nick's" happy bunch of boll weevil chasers for the summer of 1923. "Bebe" is an earnest and faithful worker and stands high in his classes. He was a member of the Track team in 1924 that won the State Championship for Clemson. Here's to you, "Bebe"; we predict great things for you in the future, and judging from your achievements among the "fair sex" nothing is impossible.

name: Harry Franklin O'Cain
motto: "We are not other than a moving row Of magic shadow shapes that come and go Round with the sun-illumin'd lantern held In midnight by the Master of the Show."
description: When Frank left home in the fall of '21, he was in quest of knowledge and it was only natural that he stopped at Clemson. At the very outset he became a real Tiger, and during his sojourn here he has become a close friend to all who know him. For some reason unknown to us, Frank decided to spend his life as a civil engineer. He must have chosen the right course, for he is one of Prof. Clarke's best students and his scholastic standing is among the highest. Frank tells us that he intends to be a bachelor. However, it is a well known fact that he worships black hair and brown eyes; and if we guess right, some wee bit of a brunette is going to play havoc with his plans. Often we see him reading a letter, with an expression of sly satisfaction beaming on his countenance, and when we ask him what it is about, he just can't explain it.

name: Dewey Leroy Outen
motto: "Stick with the crowd when it is right; but if you believe it is wrong, be man enough to do what you think is right."
description: Dewey entered our class in the fall of 1921. He is one who believes that, "To have a friend is to be one," and all who know him are aware of the fact that he has lived up to his belief. Quick to understand and sympathize with all, his many friends hate to part with him. Dewey is a hard worker and a conscientious believer in doing what he thinks is right. He is the kind of fellow that sticks to the last, and is sure to make a success in whatever he undertakes. He has been quick to make friends and keep them. Although the ladies have been one of his failings, perhaps when the fair ones are singled out to one, Dewey will, like all other great men, become a husband among husbands. May success always be yours, Dewey, in whatever task you undertake.

name: Pruitt, Ralph Samuel
motto: "If all else fail there is one profession never overcrowded; you can always be a gentleman."
description: When "Rastus" boarded the Blue Ridge "Limited" for Clemson the police force of Anderson declared a holiday for four years. He soon became tired of Clemson and his second year found him at Auburn. However, "the chickens will come home to roost" and he came back the next year. He has never completely forgotten some things in Alabama and writes letters to Auburn very frequently. Thinking that our present steam engines were in sad need of improvement "Rastus" cast his lot with the mechanical engineers and he is considered a second Kavanaugh. His winning personality together with a great amount of "stick-ability" leads us to prophesy great things for this lad.

name: Roark, Donald
motto: "Do right and fear no man. Don't write and fear no woman."
description: The people of Pickens have no idea what they lost when they allowed this illustrious son to stray from their walls. Four years ago "Sheriff" set out on the road to Clemson to astonish the world of Civil Engineering. And so with this goal in view he has become a crack-shot with the rod and transit. "Sheriff" is a good sport, loyal and true to the many friends he has won through his never failing good humor and lively disposition. Because of his friendly disposition, willing hand and optimistic spirit, we know that he will make a great success in civil life. Here's wishing you success in everything you undertake, "Sheriff," and may you some day design a cozy log cabin for your own personal use.

name: Hambright, William Anderson
motto: Smile, and the world smiles with you, frown, and you frown alone.
clubs: Secretary, Literary Critic, and Vice-President Palmetto Literary Society
Delegate Student Volunteer Convention, Indianapolis, Ind. '23
Judging Team National Dairy Show, Milwaukee, Wis. '24
description: Although "Hi-Pocket" is small of stature he is "large" in determination and is certain to attain great success in the Dairy World. Men have wondered why "Hi-Pocket" has ascended to such military heights. The whole secret lies in the fact that he lives within gun-shot of the famous King's Mountain Battle Ground. His ancestors were also great military leaders, in the Revolutionary and Civil Wars. The latter accounts for his leadership. Therefore, according to Genetics, Captain Hambright could not have been anything else but a military man, due to his heritage and environment.

name: Harden, James Cloud
motto: To be polite is to do and say the kindest things in the kindest way.
clubs: Pistol Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: "J.C." is the kind of man who makes many friends but never an enemy. His winning smile and ready laugh are a source of pleasure to all who know him. He entered Clemson with the avowed intention of becoming an Electrical Engineer. Although he has never been in danger of a nervous breakdown from over-study, he has put forth his untiring efforts to attain his objective, and right well has he succeeded. Although he may not be classed as a star athlete, he has made many others fight hard for their places on the diamond. Class work comes first with him, but 'tis rumored that he always finds time to write that fair maiden back home. We predict that Cloud's path of life will early lead up to the heights of great achievement.

name: Sease, Elton Counts
motto: A man's true love is all I have to give.
description: Two years ago this lean and lanky lad who had hitherto been just one of "Will Rogers" Civils, began to make his presence known at Clemson. In this short time he passed from a scrub football player to the top-most position among the Tiger distance runners. He became Clemson's regular one and two miler on the track team and in this capacity served his college so well that he was presented with the much coveted "C". The average track man is usually satisfied with one letter but, "Yank" working on the theory that if he could run two miles he could also run three and a half, went out for the cross-country team. Did he excel in this branch? He was elected captain of the team his senior year and after causing Tech and Georgia no little trouble led his team to the State title with a perfect score. Yank, that you may have the success in running your transit that you have had in running your team is the wish of your classmates.

name: Sease, Ralph Epting
motto: A handful of common sense is worth a bushel of learning.
description: "Chig" left the little town of Prosperity one sunny day of the year 1921 to come to Clemson. Since that day he has been trying to broaden his knowledge of Civil Engineering in order that he might improve the highways of South Carolina. There is scarcely a man at Clemson who does not know little "Chigger"; and very unfortunate is he who cannot count himself as one of his friends. This fleet-footed youngster set out in his freshman year to become a member of one of the running teams, and since that time has been a shining star in that line. Anyone who will watch him very close will think that he is a boxer, for he is constantly tackling someone who is twice as large as he is. One never sees Sease without a smile on his face. This cheerfulness along with his many other good traits will win for him many friends in his future life. Here's to you, Sease. We wish you the best of luck.

name: Wieters, Henry Carl
motto: Know enough to know that you do not know everything.
description: Truly, the city by the sea never produced a nobler and a more amiable specimen of manhood than this young gentleman who is continually looking for good in all things. Although he is not great of stature, his good qualities of character offset everything in this respect and make him an outstanding student. He has that quiet certitude, that fearlessness, that concentrated energy, and that power of direction of life, that spells out efficiency, power, and success. He has played well his part in all branches of study and we can see nothing but success awaiting him.

name: Williams, William Bratton
motto: Let the women take the initiative.
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Senior Dancing Club
description: Yes, this is Bratton Williams, the one whom you have heard so much of. He is not only known by the girls of the nearby and far communities but he is WELL known by the football teams he has played against. He is one of the best line plungers that Clemson has had in many years not only Clemson's best, but he was placed on the all-State team of 1923 as a full-back. Football is not all that he has won honors in. He is an all-American player on the team we call "damn good fellow." He can laugh, he can joke; he can play, he can work; he can love and he can like; he can do anything that goes to make a man a success. Does anyone need more than this when he steps out into the sea of life? Your home town is already proud of you and we feel sure that your state and college will justly be so in a few years.

name: Mullins, Henry David
motto: Don't think — know — then act.
description: He came to us in the fall of '21 from Gaffney. He left home to set the world on fire, and decided that the best way to accomplish this was to become a "bobbin dodger." By this rash act he became a disciple of "Swine." By his hard and conscientious work he has won many friends among the faculty and the corps. He has risen to fame in military lines, having achieved the position of cadet colonel. He is a true friend, a good student and a faithful worker. We are sure that his determination to win, his disposition to smash hearts, and his friendliness will always spell success for him. John entered the field of Textile Engineering, but the Siren Voice of Ambition called him to a profession in which there is no limit to the heights of achievements — Medicine. Go to it old fellow, rest assured of the knowledge of the whole-hearted support of your classmates and carry with you our best wishes for a successful career.

name: Smith, Malcolm McRae
motto: Start right, stay right; endings take care of themselves.
clubs: First Class Machine Gunner R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: From a "Midget" to a "Tiger" is some jump; but "Mag" managed to make the jump. When the smoke of the "Latta Branch" had cleared away in the fall of '21 and an inventory taken, it was found that "Mag" had disappeared. It was later learned that he embarked for Calhoun, later finding himself at Clemson. Malcolm entered Clemson with a scholarship, determined to have a degree in Industrial Education. He soon showed his ability as a scholar. He had no trouble being the leader in the course that he was pursuing as he was the only one exposed to that course. "Mag" has won many friends since he entered Clemson. He is liked by all who know him. He was never much of a sheik, but he seldom fails to get the "daily mail" from Lander. His favorite indoor sport is writing to Lander. With his charming personality, winning smiles, and gifted cleverness, we, the class of '25 can predict nothing but grand success in his future life. P.S.--He got his neck back.

name: Brockman, Emory Wood
motto: Kicks only raise dust and not crops from the earth.
clubs: President State Students Y. M. C. A. Council '25
Machine Gun Marksman, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: Special Student; Horticulture. "Brock," as he is called by those who know him, did not join our ranks until the fall of '23. He entered Clemson as a Sophomore, but due to his unusual ability he has been able to double-up on his work and in that way get his "sheep-skin" with the class of '25. Having traveled quite a bit before entering Clemson and having had plenty of experience, Brockman has learned to be satisfied with nothing but the best—and that he usually gets, being one of the outstanding members of his class. "Brock" decided to join the ranks of the horticulturists and aid in producing better fruits. Unlike the rest of us, he already has his "better half" so he is ready to settle down to work in his orchards near Columbia. A hail fellow well met is "Brock." His ability to mix with the fellows has won him many friends—he has no enemies—who will remember him for a long time. Here's hoping for him the best that life can give.

name: Morris, John Allen
motto: Look wise and get the benefit of a doubt.
clubs: Sophomore, Junior and Senior Dancing Clubs
description: All ye Sons of Clemson who would lay claim to supremacy in the realms of masculine pulchritude, take a look at this lad and the competition he offers. But do not think that this is all that can lay claim to, for we claim for him many distinctive features. A perfect gentleman, an earnest scholar, a true friend, an exponent of the higher things of life and the possessor of a winning personality. What more could be said of one with such attributes of success? Week-end trips show that he is not entirely unsusceptible to the charms of the fairer sex, and is very popular with the darlings. Entered the field of Textile Engineering, but the Siren Voice of Ambition called to a profession in which there is no limit to the heights of achievement—Medicine. Go to it old fellow, rest assured of the knowledge of the whole-hearted support of your classmates and carry with you our best wishes for a successful career.

name: Smith, Joseph Elihu
motto: Success is not measured by wealth alone. Nor by the amount of money we spend; But rather by realizing a great love as our own, And rendering much service to our fellow men.
clubs: Reporting Critic, President, Palmetto Literary Society
Pistol and Rifle Marksman, R.O.T.C., Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: 'Tis said that he came to Clemson with sand in his shoes, and that he thought a football coach was something to ride in; however, we did not investigate, so we do not know conclusively. By his perseverance and untiring efforts, he has reached one of his goals in life, but the end is not yet, for he intends to get a medical degree before the final goal is reached. An earnest student, a hard-worker, he never gave up a task until he had accomplished what he had set out to do. We feel sure that with such attributes, he will soon make a mark for himself in the world. The best of luck to you. The class of '25 will always cherish your memory.

name: Booker, Leonard Rowland
motto: We think too much of what others think; too little of what we ought to do.
clubs: President State Students Y. M. C. A. Council '25
Machine Gun Marksman, Camp McClellan, Ala.
description: To those who know him not, the name will mean but little; but to us who have lived with him, the name Booker will always bring to our minds a familiar saying: "Stick in there and fight 'em." Booker came to Clemson with the sole idea of making a Textile Engineer of himself, and has successfully overcome all the obstacles that the engineering faculty placed across his path. He may not become an Edison, thereby startling the world by his inventions, but his "Bull dog" tenacity and determination will put him at the top of any height he chooses to climb. His pleasing personality has won for him a host of friends whose ultimate desire is to see him succeed in whatever he undertakes.
